issue_title: Scheduler doesn't hand out unique jobs
issue_body: We have a setup where one worker runs on one instance, each with a unique identifier, and the expectation is that worker will complete all the work for that identifier. So firing off three instances with three workers (each with different identifiers) we end up with the scheduler just refusing to hand out that worker's tasks sometimes. For instance, one worker will sit around like this, with no external changes by the other pipelines being run: ``` DEBUG: Asking scheduler for work... DEBUG: Done DEBUG: There are no more tasks to run at this time DEBUG: There are 20 pending tasks possibly being run by other workers DEBUG: There are 20 pending tasks unique to this worker DEBUG: Sleeping for 5.970606 seconds DEBUG: Asking scheduler for work... DEBUG: Done DEBUG: There are no more tasks to run at this time DEBUG: There are 20 pending tasks possibly being run by other workers DEBUG: There are 20 pending tasks unique to this worker DEBUG: Sleeping for 4.901392 seconds DEBUG: Asking scheduler for work... DEBUG: Done DEBUG: There are no more tasks to run at this time DEBUG: There are 20 pending tasks possibly being run by other workers DEBUG: There are 20 pending tasks unique to this worker DEBUG: Sleeping for 5.082376 seconds DEBUG: Asking scheduler for work... DEBUG: Pending tasks: 20 2016-10-19 12:13:14,464 I [pid 8531] Worker Worker(salt=859623547, workers=1, host=ip-172-31-19-234, username=ubuntu, pid=8531, sudo_user=root) running TaskName(visit=02a41460e2454de48dcb87ef70503c19) INFO: [pid 8531] Worker Worker(salt=859623547, workers=1, host=ip-172-31-19-234, username=ubuntu, pid=8531, sudo_user=root) running TaskName(visit=02a41460e2454de48dcb87ef70503c19) ``` This delay can be seconds or minutes, with no clear indication _why_. Restarting the pipeline also seems to fix this deadlock. I feel like there's no real point for the central scheduler in our use-case, since all scheduling happens on a single instance and state is contained/communicated via file targets. For reference, this is what the workers overview looks like in the scheduler. Notice how the one worker just sits there with pending + unique pending tasks:

issue_title: Fix ignored and select settings interface with pycodestyle

issue_title: PGPMessage does not export signatures if encrypted
issue_body: Along #186, with If there are signatures on an encrypted message (i.e. encrypt-then-sign), they are not exported with the rest of the message. Although this is not necessarily the recommended order to do things in, it still constitutes a valid PGP message.

issue_title: Use “new-style” classes
issue_body:
_**Aside:** I have been waiting for an officially blessed Python parser for Gherkin for a long time.  Thank you for this!_

----

If a class has no other parents, it should explicitly inherit from `object`.

For Python 2.x, this changes the way that the Python runtime creates the class, and the runtime is optimized for new-style classes.  

It probably won’t make any noticeable difference—or, for really big test suites, it might.  I don’t know.  

In either case, it is considered good practice.

issue_title: [Feature Request] Ability to download a specific version of an S3 object
issue_body:
I want to be able to download a specific version of a file in S3.

Based on the [current documentation](http://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/s3.html#S3.Client.download_file), it seems this is not possible. Perhaps it *is* possible, but it's not documented at all.

The [documentation](http://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/s3.html#S3.Client.download_file) says:

> Similar behavior as S3Transfer's download_file() method, except that parameters are capitalized. Detailed examples can be found at S3Transfer's Usage.

But the docs for "S3Transfer" are not linked to anywhere. The [github README](https://github.com/boto/s3transfer) for S3Transfer just says to use boto3.

My current workaround is to:

* manually download the version I want with a browser on my local machine
* manually upload that under a new key
* use boto3 or terminal client to download the file from that new key, on my headless server

issue_title: Issues moving large files
issue_body:
There was a previous issue that briefly discussed this, but was resolved.  am trying to unzip and transfer files between two S3 buckets using lambda. The issue is that these are bulk loaded files and are rather large, some can get up to 15GB. Right now I have to write everything into memory and so the function will fail, has there been any additions for streaming that could help fix this? The other idea I had was having the ability to attach a temporary EBS volume or along those lines to work as a temporary fix, but unclear if this is possible. 

```
import json
import urllib
import boto3
from boto3.s3.transfer import S3Transfer
from zipfile import ZipFile as zip


print('Loading function')

s3 = boto3.client('s3')
transfer = S3Transfer(s3)
dump_bucket = ''

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    print("Received event: " + json.dumps(event, indent=2))

    # Get the object from the event and show its content type
    bucket = event['Records'][0]['s3']['bucket']['name']
    key = urllib.unquote_plus(event['Records'][0]['s3']['object']['key']).decode('utf8')
    try:
        response = s3.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key)

        s3.download_file(bucket, key, '/tmp/file.zip')
        zippedfile = zip('/tmp/file.zip')
        namelist = zippedfile.namelist()

        for filename in namelist:
            data = zfile.read(filename)
            f = open('/tmp/' + str(filename), 'wb')
            f.write(data)
            f.close()

        transfer.upload_file('/tmp/' + str(filename), dump_bucket, namelist[0])
    except Exception as e:
        print(e)
        print('Error getting object {} from bucket {}. Make sure they exist and your bucket is in the same region as this function.'.format(key, bucket))
        raise e
```

issue_title: [Feature request] Ability to fetch full cloudwatch logs from boto after lambda invoke
issue_body:
I would really like to be able to fetch cloudwatch logs from boto3. 
As in, I want a way to fetch in python what I can see at:

    https://ap-southeast-2.console.aws.amazon.com/cloudwatch/home?region=ap-southeast-2#logEventViewer:group=/aws/lambda/my_lambda_name;start=PT5M

As far as I can tell, there's no way to do this with boto3.

I can get close by looking at `base64.b64decode(response['LogResult'])` after calling `response = boto3.client('lambda', config=config).invoke(...)`.

However those logs are only a subset of the actual logs.

The specific case I'm dealing with is looking at logs of lambdas which execute python `nose` tests. In the web GUI for CloudWatch I see:

* Traceback (exception captured, handled and printed by nose, I don't know exactly how);
* Logger output; then
* stdout

With `base64.b64decode(response['LogResult'])` I only see logger output, not the other 2.

Is there a way to see the other 2 from boto3? If not, I would like there to be. My current workaround is to run `client.invoke()` and then generate the aforementioned url so the user can copy and paste it into their browser. This is a pain because (aside from being manual), when multiple lambdas are invoked in parallel, the logs become very confusing, and it takes a while to find what I'm actually looking for.

In addition to modifying `client.invoke` so that it returns the full logs, I think it would be generally useful to add a function to the CloudWatch client so that you can look up the logs for a particular lambda, or a particular invocation of a particular lambda.

``` python
blob = repo.revparse_single(commit + ':' + path)
result = blob.data
```

GitPython code:

``` python
blob = repo.rev_parse(commit + ':' + path)
```

issue_title: Failure with unintended side-effects, while reconfiguring a Distributed vSwitch
issue_body:
Environment:

vCenter 6.5
pyVmomi 6.5
vconnector (0.4.6)
Python 2.7.13

I have a script which connects to vCenter and updates the discovery protocol settings on all dvSwitches.  

Here is a snippet that illustrates what the code is doing.  Basically it finds dvSwitches that need updates and then it creates a new `LinkDiscoveryProtocolConfig` and sets the protocol to `ProtocolType.lldp`, and then it updates the dvswitch with the new configuration.


```python
 def enable_lldp_advertise(self, dvswitch):
        """Enable LLDP advertising settings on a Distributed vSwitch.

        Arguments:
            dvswitch (DistributedVirtualSwitch): A distributed vSwitch.

        Returns:
             bool: True if the update is successful. False otherwise.
        """
        protocol_config = vim.host.LinkDiscoveryProtocolConfig()
        protocol_config.protocol = vim.host.LinkDiscoveryProtocolConfig.ProtocolType.lldp
        return self._enable_link_discovery_advertise(dvswitch, protocol_config)

    def _enable_link_discovery_advertise(self, dvswitch, protocol_config):
        """Enable Link Discovery Protocol advertising settings on a Distributed vSwitch.

        Arguments:
            dvswitch (DistributedVirtualSwitch): A distributed vSwitch.
            protocol_config (LinkDiscoveryProtocolConfig): Configuration specifying the selected
                Link Discovery Protocol to use for this switch.

        Returns:
            bool: True if the update is successful. False otherwise.
        """
        if self.is_advertise_enabled(dvswitch):
            # advertise is enabled, don't change the setting
            protocol_config.operation = dvswitch.config.linkDiscoveryProtocolConfig.operation
        elif self.is_listen_enabled(dvswitch):
            # listen is enabled, add advertise by setting to both
            protocol_config.operation = vim.host.LinkDiscoveryProtocolConfig.OperationType.both
        else:
            # nothing is enabled, set to advertise
            protocol_config.operation = vim.host.LinkDiscoveryProtocolConfig.OperationType.advertise

        config_spec = vim.dvs.VmwareDistributedVirtualSwitch.ConfigSpec(
            configVersion=dvswitch.config.configVersion,
            linkDiscoveryProtocolConfig=protocol_config)
        task = dvswitch.ReconfigureDvs_Task(spec=config_spec)

        return vsphere_utils.wait_for_task(task=task,
                                           timeout=30,
                                           logger=self._logger)
```

The `vsphere_utils.wait_for_task` is implemented as:

```python
from pyVmomi import vim

def wait_for_task(task, timeout, logger):
    """Wait for a Task in vSphere to run to completion.

    Arguments:
        task (Task): The vSphere Task.
        timeout (int): Maximum amount of time in seconds to wait for the task to complete
        logger (logger): For logging updates

    Returns:
         bool: True is the Task was successful. False otherwise.
    """
    running = vim.TaskInfo.State.running
    queued = vim.TaskInfo.State.queued

    start = time.time()
    while task.info.state == running or task.info.state == queued:
        if time.time() - start > timeout:
            logger.warning('%s second timeout exceeded while waiting for Task to complete', timeout)
            break
        logger.info('Task running...')
        time.sleep(1)

    if task.info.state == vim.TaskInfo.State.error:
        logger.critical(task.info.error.msg)

    return task.info.state == vim.TaskInfo.State.success
```


I am investigating an issue where this code resulted in the dis-association of a set of Physical NICs from the active interfaces associated with the Dv_uplinks on a dvswitch.  I don't see how that could occur based on the data model, but I am curious about the failure mode for `ReconfigureDvs_Task` method.  During the execution, the code received the following exception:

```
ERROR:vsphere.EnableDiscoveryProtocolOnDistributedVSwitch:Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/opt/vsphere/actions/../lib/vsphere_connector.py", line 60, in connect
    self.connect_raise_exceptions(username, password, host, verify_ssl)
  File "/opt/vsphere/actions/../lib/vsphere_connector.py", line 98, in connect_raise_exceptions
    self.vsphere_connection.connect()
  File "/opt/virtualenvs/vsphere/lib/python2.7/site-packages/vconnector/core.py", line 159, in connect
    sslContext=self.ssl_context,
  File "/opt/virtualenvs/vsphere/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyVim/connect.py", line 663, in SmartConnect
    sslContext)
  File "/opt/virtualenvs/vsphere/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyVim/connect.py", line 552, in __FindSupportedVersion
    sslContext)
  File "/opt/virtualenvs/vsphere/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyVim/connect.py", line 472, in __GetServiceVersionDescription
    tree = __GetElementTreeFromUrl(url, sslContext)
  File "/opt/virtualenvs/vsphere/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyVim/connect.py", line 438, in __GetElementTreeFromUrl
    sock = requests.get(url, verify=False)
  File "/opt/virtualenvs/vsphere/lib/python2.7/site-packages/requests/api.py", line 70, in get
    return request(\'get\', url, params=params, **kwargs)
  File "/opt/virtualenvs/vsphere/lib/python2.7/site-packages/requests/api.py", line 56, in request
    return session.request(method=method, url=url, **kwargs)
  File "/opt/virtualenvs/vsphere/lib/python2.7/site-packages/requests/sessions.py", line 488, in request
    resp = self.send(prep, **send_kwargs)
  File "/opt/virtualenvs/vsphere/lib/python2.7/site-packages/requests/sessions.py", line 609, in send
    r = adapter.send(request, **kwargs)
  File "/opt/virtualenvs/vsphere/lib/python2.7/site-packages/requests/adapters.py", line 487, in send
    raise ConnectionError(e, request=request)
ConnectionError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host=\’vcsa65.plexxi.com\’, port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: //sdk/vimServiceVersions.xml (Caused by NewConnectionError(\'<requests.packages.urllib3.connection.VerifiedHTTPSConnection object at 0x7f1b041a2ad0>: Failed to establish a new connection: [Errno -2] Name or service not known\',))
ERROR:root:Cannot connect to vcsa65.plexxi.com: HTTPSConnectionPool(host=\'vcsa65.plexxi.com\', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: //sdk/vimServiceVersions.xml (Caused by NewConnectionError(\'<requests.packages.urllib3.connection.VerifiedHTTPSConnection object at 0x7f1b040b08d0>: Failed to establish a new connection: [Errno -2] Name or service not known\',))
```

My theory is that the DNS configuration changed on the host while this`ReconfigureDvs_Task` was in progress, and that resulted in the HTTPSConnectionPool to fail to make additional connections.  My primary question is what will happen to vSphere if this happens?  Is there a transactional rollback mechanism that will undo any of the changes that `ReconfigureDvs_Task` initiated, or will this potentially leave the system in a mis-configured state?

I found a similar issue [HERE](https://github.com/vmware/pyvmomi-community-samples/issues/239) with requests/urllib3 HTTPSConnectionPool related to file descriptors, yet the error: `[Errno -2] Name or service not known` in this case suggests DNS resolution errors.

issue_title: Send Money Transaction Details
issue_body:
### General Information

- Mode (Sandbox/Live): Live
- Version of Python used: 2.7.11
- SDK Version: 1.11.7

### Issue Description

There appears to be no way with the API to access needed information about money sent to a user via 'Send Money' on the PayPal website. This is often used to refund an unsatisfied customer after the ability to refund the original transaction has expired.

Currently the transaction ID which PayPal associates with these transactions can only be looked up via the `Sale` endpoint, which provides frustrating little information. Compared to the web view on the PayPal website there is almost no detail regarding the transaction. Most frustratingly PayPal recently changed to showing the amount sent in the local currency, and there is no way to get from the information returned on the `Sale` endpoint to the amount in USD which was actually used to fund the transaction.

This is a gaping hole in the current API and extremely frustrating. Is there any plan to fix this gap and provide the same information as is seen on the web view?

issue_title: "Meet the Repo type" example improvement
issue_body:
New to this library and just want to ask for feedback before submitting a PR.  

The "Meet the Repo type" example below shows how to create an instance of `Repo`, but it does so by depending on an already created `Repo` instance in some bespoke outer scope.  It then spends 3 lines of comments explaining the what `rorepo` is, and that it's completely unnecessary for the example.  Most importantly, the user cannot simply copy and paste the example and run it locally. 

```
from git import Repo

# rorepo is a Repo instance pointing to the git-python repository.
# For all you know, the first argument to Repo is a path to the repository
# you want to work with
repo = Repo(self.rorepo.working_tree_dir)
assert not repo.bare
```

Is there a reason this example is not something simpler like below?
```
import os
from git import Repo

repo = Repo(os.getcwd())
assert not repo.bare
```

issue_title: Delete checkbox doesn't work in Django 1.11
`Get Cookies` should support returning cookies as objects and have better logging
issue_body:
Currently `Get Cookies` returns a cookies as a string in format `cookie1=value1; cookie2=value2`. This isn't very useful if you need to actually handle all cookies somehow, and the return format isn't even documented anywhere. We should change the keyword to return cookies as a list (or ordered dictionary) of CookieInformation objects that were introduced as part of #932.

Changing keyword return value is obviously backwards incompatible, so we cannot do it without a deprecation period. I propose that in v3.1 we add an argument like `return_objects=False` that can be used to explicitly change the return value. This allows users to opt-in if they want to get objects. In v3.2 we could then change the argument to `return_objects=True`, making it possible to opt-out if needed. And in v3.3 or later the whole argument can be removed. This issue only covers adding `return_objects=False` (or similar), changing and removing it later require separate issues later.

Another problem in `Get Cookies` is that it doesn't log anything. The beginning of the return value is logged automatically if the return value is assigned to a variable, but it would be better to log at least names and values explicitly.

---

**UPDATE:** @Mohorelien commented below that there actually is a use case for the current format in HTTP request headers. I still think having a way to return cookie information as objects is useful, but we should preserver the current functionality too. I'd also keep the current functionality as the default functionality to avoid all backwards incompatible changes. That way this change could even be done in SL 3.0 if we have time.

issue_title: Possible legal problems with vendor/patched libraries
issue_body:
# tl;dr

Pipenv probably violates couple of free software licenses by not shipping their text. It is also probably "infected" by GPL.

# Details

Hello, we (@mcyprian, me and the Fedora Python SIG @fedora-python) are trying to finally package pipenv for Fedora, so user can just do `sudo dnf install pipenv`. The Fedora package review request is in [Red Hat Bugzilla 1564500](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1564500).

While we are trying to unbundle (unvendor?) most of the 3rd party libraries shipped with pipenv, we are in a bit of hurry so we decided to leave the libraries that are

 * not yet packaged for Fedora,
 * or patched

bundled for now. The first category is a TODO for future, the second will probably remain bundled forever. 

As part of the review of the Fedora package, the reviewer is obligated to check whether the package is licensed with [approved free software/content license](https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Licensing:Main?rd=Licensing#Good_Licenses) and whether the licensing information for the package is correct.

## Missing licenses

This is where I found out that all the vendored 3rd party libraries are shipped without their LICENSE/COPYING/etc. files and the [NOTICES](https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/master/NOTICES) file is shipped instead.

> The contents of the vendor and patched directories are subject to different licenses
> than the rest of this project. Their respective licenses can be looked
> up at pypi.python.org.

This is unacceptable for Fedora\* and IMHO should not be acceptable for @pypa either. Most of the libraries are licensed with licenses that **require** the license text to be shipped. See MIT:

> The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in all
> copies or substantial portions of the Software.

Or BSD:

> Redistributions of source code must retain the above copyright notice, this
>  list of conditions and the following disclaimer.

This is similar for most of the permissive licenses. *You should not strip the license file, in fact you need to ship it with the code unconditionally.* You may put the license text and copyrights inside another file, such as NOTICES, but a link to pypi is IMHO not enough.

\* This is currently only my opinion. It has not yet been reviewed by the Fedora legal team.

## Copyleft

Not to mention that [strict_rfc3339](https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/master/pipenv/vendor/strict_rfc3339.py) is shipped under the terms of the GNU General Public License version 3 or later. Needless to say that GPL is a copyleft license. By bundling this part of code inside pipenv, pipenv is "infected" with this license and shall be GPLv3 as well (which I think is undesired).

IANAL, however I'm quite confident that pipenv now violates couple of free software licenses including the GPL. This currently blocks us from inclusion into Fedora. Since [pipenv is the recommended tool](https://packaging.python.org/tutorials/managing-dependencies/) I think this shall be brought to @pypa.

# Conclusion

What I believe shall be done:

 * The license text shall be reintroduced, either individually or in one file.
 * Pipenv shall loose any copyleft licensed dependencies if it wishes to remain licensed under MIT.
 
I offer my help with collecting the licenses back, if that's agreed upon by pipenv maintainers. I can also try to replace strict_rfc3339 with [rfc3339](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/rfc3339), however I haven't looked into it yet. There might also be other copylefted files (without a header that makes it obvious).

```
name = "artifactory"
```

```
ARTIFACTORY_USERNAME=myuser
```

issue_title: Where to find environment configuration defaults
issue_body:
While getting started with `pipenv` and reading thorough the documentation, I came across [_Configuration With Environment Variables_](https://docs.pipenv.org/advanced/#configuration-with-environment-variables).

Unfortunately, some of the items don't list their default value, and I'm not quite sure how to find it. So while I'm initially curious if anyone could point me in the right direction, I also think it would be useful to newcomers if they were listed in the section linked above.

issue_title: Keeping pipenv up-to-date with python versions seems like a lot of work
issue_body:
On this line right here, there's a mapping dictionary: https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/master/pipenv/core.py#L503

However, since it's using pyenv, maybe we should adopt an approach closer to this: https://github.com/concordusapps/pyenv-implict

issue_title: License clarification
issue_body: Sorry, I think I'm missing something really obvious and could use some help understanding. I think `setuptools` use to be licensed with PSF or ZPL. However, I do not actually see a license file here. All I see is a [classifier]( https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/cb0a2aeb86953d6b3cf6f039bb887fb8c7e8d1e9/setup.py#L132 ) that suggests this is MIT. Is this MIT or something else? Also could we please have a license file here to make that a bit clearer? Thanks.

pip v10 ships with distlib v0.2.6, which introduced a clever way to work around the shebang limit on certain systems. The problem is that this shebang isn't recognized at all by the `--relocatable` flag.

```python
        else:
            result = b'#!/bin/sh\n'
            result += b"'''exec' " + executable + post_interp + b' "$0" "$@"\n'
            result += b"' '''"
        return result
```

https://github.com/pypa/pip/blob/87d2735487fe2e8296d777c43ecb9920a2201bd6/src/pip/_vendor/distlib/scripts.py#L164-L168

Here's an example of what it looks like:

```
#!/bin/sh

' '''
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import re
import sys

from foobar import main

if __name__ == '__main__':

```

issue_title: Optimization: avoid using catalog

When the simplelayout provider is redered, at least two catalog queries are executed:

https://github.com/4teamwork/ftw.simplelayout/blob/b303a6ecb037eb00da3c3999cf390c7f392e34c4/ftw/simplelayout/browser/provider.py#L146-L156
(once in `_blocks_without_state`).

Since all block objects are loaded anyways, we can just load the objects and do not ask the catalog.

```python
blocks = filter(has_view_permission, filter(ISimplelayoutBlock.providedBy, page.objectValues()))
```

We should measure that in order to prove that the peformance is better.

issue_title: Can't parse valid date
default time zones of input data
issue_body:
Hey! Why does dateparser convert date *to* timezone specified in settings, not *from* this timezone? 

For example, let's say there is a forum with UTC+5 timezone, so all dates are in UTC+5, and I want to have dates in UTC. Passing TIMEZONE='UTC+5' won't help - dateparser will assume dates are in UTC, and then convert them to UTC+5, i.e. it'll add five hours instead of subtracting five hours.

Once you have a date in a known timezone it is easy to convert it to any other timezone using other libraries, you don't need dateparser support for this. So currently 'TIMEZONE' option is merely a helper. 

The problem is that it is tricky to handle relative dates correctly with dateparser now. Let's say we're on that forum again, with UTC+5 dates. Old dates are written in full, e.g. "2016-09-07 12:30", recent dates are relative, e.g. "1 hour ago".

Without timezone information dateparser will think both dates are in UTC. It means it'll use utcnow() as a current time when computing a full date for "1 hour ago", which is not what they meant on a website.

Let's say current time (as used by website) is 2016-09-07 15:00 UTC+5, or 2016-09-07 10:00 UTC.

text  | date in UTC+5 | date in UTC | parse result
-----|---------------|------------- | ------------
1 hour ago | 2016-09-07 14:00 | 2016-09-07 09:00 | 2016-09-07 09:00 UTC
2016-09-07 12:30 | 2016-09-07 12:30 | 2016-09-07 07:30 | 2016-09-07 12:30 UTC

As you see, 'parse result' is incorrect. TIMEZONE option doesn't help here because both times are adjusted by the same amount.

`RELATIVE_BASE` can help, but the code is far from obvious:

```py
from datetime import datetime
import pytz

forum_tz = pytz.timezone("UTC+5")  # forum time zone

# current time as tz-aware UTC datetime
now_utc = pytz.utc.localize(datetime(2016, 9, 7, 10)) 

# here goes a hack: even though docs say 
# "Dateparser assumes all dates to be in UTC",
# we have to pass RELATIVE_BASE as a naive (not tz-aware) 
# datetime in UTC+5 timezone
forum_now = now_utc.astimezone(forum_tz)  # current time in forum timezone
settings = {
   'RELATIVE_BASE': forum_now.replace(tzinfo=None),
}
dt = dateparser.parse(date_string, settings=settings)

# the returned dt is not in UTC, it is in UTC+5. Convert it back to UTC:
dt = forum_tz.localize(dt).astimezone(pytz.utc)
```

What do you think about adding DEFAULT_INPUT_TIMEZONE option to handle this use case? Maybe it also worths it to rename 'TIMEZONE' option to 'RESULT_TIMEZONE', in order to avoid confusion.

issue_title: Validate resource definitions
issue_body:
This is a proposal of an idea that I'd like to implement but want some advice beforehand.

We could use @garethr's [kubernetes json schema definitions](https://github.com/garethr/kubernetes-json-schema) to validate kubernetes resource definitions prior to calling `create`, `patch` or `replace`.

The license is Apache 2.0 which is the same as this repository, so there are no licensing issues associated with just using the definitions locally. However, it could cause the size of the library to increase.

So I guess my questions are:
* Should we just build a useful subset of resource definitions to include with the client
* Should we turn the resource definitions into a separate python module suitable for optionally installing alongside the client
* Should we just download the appropriate schema from the internet (i.e. github in this case) when a validation is requested and cache somewhere appropriate
* Is this change more appropriate for the kubernetes client than the openshift client? Or is it something that should be the responsibility of the openshift client caller?

issue_title: Strange regression with some unicode characters (e.g. with the Russian Н)
issue_body:
pyte 0.6 has a strange regression with some Unicode characters, particularly with the Russian "Н" character:

That works:

```
$ cat  regression.py
# vim: encoding=utf-8
import sys
import pyte

text = "Русский текст"
screen = pyte.screens.Screen(20, 1)
stream = pyte.streams.ByteStream()
stream.attach(screen)
stream.feed(text)

for line in screen.buffer:
    for x in line:

    sys.stdout.write("\n")

$ python regression.py
Русский текст
```

issue_title: The edit_on_github extension should only work for master
issue_body:
*Originally reported by @bsipocz in https://github.com/astropy/astropy-helpers/issues/316*

When people are opening PRs using the edit_on_github feature it most usually causes a bigger mess than it solves as most usually they open the PR against the wrong branch (see the latest issue here against "stable": astropy/astropy#6172)

I suggest to try to disable it for anything but the "latest" rtd version. I think the other option of making it point always to the master version will be more confusing, as the page may have changed significantly since the given version one tries to edit.

The other workaround could be to train astrobot to pick up all PRs that are not against master and comment on them prior merge.

Having a closer look, it seems to be that pointing the edit_on_github link always to master is easy and only needs the modification in docs/conf.py in the astropy repo. The question is whether that is a good approach (I think yes, it's definitely better than dealing with the incorrect base branch in PRs).

cc @eteq @astrofrog

issue_title: Improve error handling in get_package_info
issue_body:
As demonstrated in astropy/astropy#3801 (and other past build problems), sometimes odd exceptions can crop up from various submodules while running setup.py (and in particular the `get_package_info` that will actually import numerous submodules).

Often these have to do with user misconfiguration or an unclean build environment.  It would be good to handle such exceptions in a way that informs the user of as much (with some possible hints for resolving the issue--these would of course be generic tips and wouldn't cover every possible problem).  This would be preferable to potentially arbitrary tracebacks that, as far as the user is concerned, indicate a bug.

issue_title: Strange behavior of additional_owners field in Connect Custom accounts
issue_body:
I found two behaviors quite strange when using the library, including something that may be a bug in the library.

**Issue 1**
When creating a Custom account, `additional_owners` cannot be passed as an array even though that's the format the API outputs, see code below:
```python
legal_entity_data = { 'first_name': 'something', 'additional_owners': [{ 'first_name': 'LLL' }] }
account_token = stripe.Token.create( account = { 'legal_entity': legal_entity_data, 'tos_shown_and_accepted': True })
# ^
# Raises an InvalidRequestError with message 'Invalid hash'
# But it works if we use { 0: { 'first_name' }: 'LLL' } ...
```

**Issue 2**
I can't remove any formerly created `additional_owners` by deleting the property on the Stripe account object as per https://stripe.com/docs/connect/account-tokens (though given there is no code example I may be interpreting the doc in the wrong way in which case would love to know what the right syntax is)

```python
legal_entity_data = { 'first_name': 'something', 'additional_owners': { 0: { 'first_name': 'LLL' }} }
account_token = stripe.Token.create( account = { 'legal_entity': legal_entity_data, 'tos_shown_and_accepted': True })
stripe_account = stripe.Account.create(type = 'custom', country = 'FR', account_token = account_token.id)

legal_entity_data = { 'first_name': 'another' }   #, 'additional_owners': None }
account_token = stripe.Token.create( account = { 'legal_entity': legal_entity_data, 'tos_shown_and_accepted': True })
stripe_account = stripe.Account.retrieve(stripe_account['id'])   # Should be idempotent but just in case
stripe_account.account_token = account_token.id

del stripe_account.legal_entity.additional_owners
# ^
# This raises a KeyError even though the attribute does exit
# Same if I use stripe_account['legal_entity']['additional_owners']
# Btw being able to access properties both as attributes and dict keys smells a lot like magic
# and there seems to be some in `stripe_object.py`, from where the KeyError originates

stripe_account.save()
stripe_account = stripe.Account.retrieve(stripe_account['id'])   # Should be idempotent but just in case
assert(stripe_account['legal_entity']['first_name'] == 'another')   # Works, means the update did happen
assert(len(stripe_account['legal_entity']['additional_owners']) == 0)

```

issue_title: Stripe-python's APIRequestor takes an optional client, but there's no way to pass it in
issue_body:
APIRequestor defaults to creating a new http client on each request (since it is constructed by the external-api methods without a client being passed in).

This means, for example, the `requests` connection pooling can't be used, and a new SSL handshake is needed for each API call.

Please add a way to pass in an existing client, similar to what you do for `api_key` without setting `stripe.api_key`.

    stripe.Customer.create(..., http_client=client)

for example, would do.

Then you could document using `http_client.new_default_http_client` as the default, which would give people enough clue to pass in a client if they wanted something different.

issue_title: Allow Fireworks to reference data held in Workflow
issue_body:
I run a lot of workflows where I have a template which I modify for each individual Firework in a Workflow.  This leads to a lot of bloat in the Launchpad (ie the mongodb).

Ideally I'd be allowed to do something like this:
```python
fw1 = fw.Firework([Task1(), Task2()], fw_spec={'template': WorkflowStatic('template')})
fw2 = fw.Firework([Task1(), Task2()], fw_spec={'template': WorkflowStatic('template')})

fw.Workflow([fw1,fw2], statics={'template': [1, 2, 3, 4]})
```
So `template` is defined once in the Workflow, and Fireworks can refer to it in their spec.

In terms of implementing this, after a quick poke around, I think I could do it by changing `Launchpad.get_fw_dict_by_id` to scan for the `WorkflowStatic` objects and replace these with the corresponding document from the Workflow.

So questions..
 - has this been thought of before? (and is it a bad idea?)
 - is it enough to modify `get_fw_dict_by_id` or are there other points that a Firework is accessed?

issue_title: io.imsave(name, array, plugin='simpleitk') not working correctly for Nifti 3D images
issue_body:
When saving a 3D image in NIfTI format (.nii) using skimage.io.imsave() and plugin 'simpleitk', the result image cannot be read correctly from other softwares. For instance Fiji loads X dimension as channels, therefore the Y and Z dimensions becomes X and Y.

The problems is that the NIfTI header is incorrect and indicate a 5 dimensions image (xyztc) instead of a 3 dimensions image (xyz).

## Source of the error
The code for this function is:
```
def imsave(fname, arr):
    sitk_img = sitk.GetImageFromArray(arr, isVector=True)
    sitk.WriteImage(sitk_img, fname)
```

"isVector=True" is responsible for the problem, as it force a 3D array to be treated as a 2D vector image (from SimpleITK doc, as cited below)

> If isVector is True, then a 3D array will be treaded as a 2D vector image, otherwise it will be treaded as a 3D image.


I suggest two options:

1. create an argument to force "isVector=True", which will differenciate between 2D multi-channel and 3D images.

2. create "volsave()" that would consider that 3D arrays are 3D images by default.


## Way to reproduce
The code below should allow you to reproduce the issue.
Afterward, open images in ImageJ/Fiji to see the difference.

```
import skimage
import SimpleITK as sitk

# Place your 3D image path here
a = skimage.io.imread(path_to_your_3D_image)
# Place your save path here
p = r'your_save_path'

path_to_nifti_imsave = p + r'\image_imsave.nii'
path_to_nifti_sitk_isVector = p + r'\image_sitk_isVector.nii'
path_to_nifti_sitk = p + r'\image_sitk.nii'

skimage.io.imsave(path_to_nifti_imsave, a, plugin='simpleitk')

sitk.WriteImage(GetImageFromArray(a, isVector=True), path_to_nifti_sitk_isVector)

sitk.WriteImage(GetImageFromArray(a), path_to_nifti_sitk)
```

issue_title: HOG visualization should use midpoints of bins
issue_body:
## Description
The HOG visualization produces lines that are perpendicular to the edges of orientation bins.  It would be clearer if the lines were perpendicular to the _midpoints_ of bins, instead of the edges.

For example, if `hog` is applied with two orientation bins (`orientations=2`) to the image on the left below (reproducible by the code below), I would have expected the visualization to produce something looking like "concentric diamonds."  But it instead looks like the image to its right.

![comparison](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5924638/21959910/293b0192-da8b-11e6-8ba9-c72c5085a368.png)

If others agree that this would be a worthwhile change, I'd be happy to work on a pull request for it.

## Way to reproduce
Here is the code to reproduce the images above:
```python
import numpy as np
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt
from skimage.feature import hog

X,Y = np.mgrid[-256:256,-256:256]
image = np.sqrt(X**2 + Y**2)
_, hog_image = hog(
    image,
    orientations=2,
    pixels_per_cell=(32, 32),
    cells_per_block=(1, 1),
    visualise=True
)
fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 8))
ax1.imshow(image, cmap=plt.cm.gray, interpolation='nearest')
ax2.imshow(hog_image, cmap=plt.cm.gray, interpolation='nearest')
```
(I'm using Python 3.5.2, scikit-image 0.12.3)

issue_title: Maintenance: More simplification of `__init__.py`
issue_body:
## Description
In my mind, `__init__.py` is very cluttered. Here is a breakdown of the lines of code in `__init__.py`.

Happy to do PRs for any of the suggestions that get approved.

| Lines | Purpose | Fraction of file | Alternative |
|-------|---------|--------|--------|
| 1-57  | Documentation   | large| Keep, this is great!  |
| 59, 65-66 | data_dir  | small  | ☑ [data_dir] Fixed in  #3161 |
| 68   | version  |   | Keep, this is great!  |
| 71-88   | python version  |  medium  | ☑ [python version] Fixed in #3160 |
| 91-131 | Test  |  large | ☑ [pytest] Fixed in #3164 |
| 134-178 | `__SKIMAGE_SETUP__` hack |  large | ☐ Addressed in #3158  |
| 181-192 | Numpy cross/docs | medium | ☑ [lookfor] Fixed in #3162 |

With refactoring, the file would look like:

```python
"""Docs
[...]
"""
__version__ = "0.15.dev0"

from .util.dtype import *
from .data import data_dir

from .util import lookfor
```

### [datadir] 
I suggest:
  - `from .data import data_dir`
  - Or just tell users to `from skimage.data import data_dir as skimge_datadir` as they are only likely to use this in the initial tutorial phase of their work.

### [python version] 
I think this should be removed.
Also, this problem shouldn't happen much most users will be installing scikit-image from pip/conda where 0.15 won't even exist in python2 repos.

Alternatively, it should point to `http://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/install.html` and not duplicate the instructions IMO.

### [pytest] 
Instead of having our own wrapper for pytest, just tell users to run the pytest command themselves.

From the terminal: `python -m pytest --pyargs skimage`
From a python console: `import pytest; pytest.main('--pyargs skimage')`

The reasoning is:
  1. It never worked before. See PR #3152.
  2. It was never tested before #3152.
  3. I don't think you should have wrappers for 1 liners.

### [lookfor] 
This one is tricky because it uses introspection.

Maybe move to `skimage.utils.docs` but it uses introspection, so the code would have to be rewritten too.

issue_title: Remove Python shebang / executable bits
issue_body:
Currently, our Python convention is that any executable Python scripts should have the following:

1. An `if __name__ == "__main__":` block
2. First line should be `#!/usr/bin/env python`
3. File should have executable bit set

This proposal is to remove steps 2 and 3 since they are unnecessary and add extra steps. Plus the "entry_points" option in the `setup.py` script allows you to specify files or functions that should be installed as executable scripts, bypassing the need for steps 2 and 3.

issue_title: Why does smoke-test.sh pull a fixed version of e2e.py from kinto-signer?
issue_body:
smoke-test.sh contains the following code:

```sh
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kinto/kinto-signer/1.3.0/scripts/e2e.py
```

Note that the version of `e2e.py` is taken from `kinto-signer` 1.3.0. However, later in the file is:

```sh
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kinto/kinto-signer/master/scripts/validate_signature.py
```

My questions are:

1. Why 1.3.0 instead of master? Is this meant to be a "known good" version? Is it supposed to represent existing code "in the wild"?
2. For that matter, why master instead of some "latest release"?
3. Actually, why do we `curl` these files at all rather than i.e. extracting them from installed packages?

```yaml
excludes:
```

issue_title: Extending `CELERYBEAT_SCHEDULE`
issue_body:
It appears that there is currently no way to add a key to the [CELERYBEAT_SCHEDULE](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-celery/blob/master/invenio_celery/config.py#L97) variable, instead of completely overwriting it (with no knowledge of what we're overwriting).

Such functionality would enable modules to declare what they want to add to the beat, instead of the administrator of the overlay being responsible of creating this schedule on their own.

Example use-case:
The future `checker` requires being able to run tasks periodically, and adding these periodic tasks to celery beat is the closest-to-native-invenio solution we could come up with.

issue_title: Download all inputs regression due to PTFM-18767
issue_body:
Changes from [PTFM-18767](https://github.com/dnanexus/dx-toolkit/blob/90902a101935b30f53659a033e5d5c6ddd976505/src/python/dxpy/bindings/download_all_inputs.py#L189-L194) results in usage of `dxpy.download_all_inputs(parallel=True)` to hang and slowly eat available memory.

`max_threads` is still an optional parameter that [is not set to a default value](https://github.com/dnanexus/dx-toolkit/commit/90902a101935b30f53659a033e5d5c6ddd976505#diff-d9c1c9ff7742a1569532d53081ead50cL182) before being used in [`min()`](https://github.com/dnanexus/dx-toolkit/commit/90902a101935b30f53659a033e5d5c6ddd976505#diff-d9c1c9ff7742a1569532d53081ead50cR112) to find the number of parallel workers. As a result Number of workers is always [set to None](https://github.com/dnanexus/dx-toolkit/blob/90902a101935b30f53659a033e5d5c6ddd976505/src/python/dxpy/bindings/download_all_inputs.py#L63).

Probably add the default value for `max_threads` back. Perhaps handle `max_workers=0` for ThreadPoolExecutor constructor?

issue_title: OrderedContainer.updateOrder corrupts keys
issue_body:
It doesn't ensure the incoming keys are text, leading to possible corruption. Here's Python 2:
```pycon
>>> from zope.container.ordered import OrderedContainer
>>> oc = OrderedContainer()
>>> oc['a'] = 1
>>> oc['b'] = 2
>>> oc.keys()
[u'a', u'b']
>>> oc.updateOrder(('b', 'a')) # Note the bytes
None
>>> oc.keys() # Unicode is gone!
['b', 'a'] 
```

Python 3 fails:
```pycon
>>> c.updateOrder((b'b', b'a',))
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "//lib/python3.6/site-packages/zope/container/ordered.py", line 285, in updateOrder
    raise ValueError("Incompatible key set.")
ValueError: Incompatible key set.
```

I think it should either run the new keys through the same name conversion that happens in `__setitem__` OR it should not accept non-unicode keys.

I encountered this in `zope.copypastemove` tests.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Could we add back the dependency on zope.cachedescriptors?
issue_body:
This package duplicates `readproperty` and `Lazy`, without some of their newer bug fixes/enhancements.

zope.cachedescriptors has no dependencies other than setuptools.

I see that this dependency was removed back in 2009. I understand the desire to minimize dependencies, but I also see the benefit to sharing the implementation of such things, especially when they are as low-level as non-data descriptors.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Is it possible to move the .buildozer folder somewhere else?
issue_body:
I have a Python 2 virtual environment where I installed kivy and buildozer. My virtualenv is kept separated from the project folder in a dedicated folder in `~/.virtualenvs`. I tried buildozer today and I noticed that it created two folders: 1) `~/.buildozer` , and 2) `PROJ/.buildozer` , where `PROJ` is the project directory. Is it possible to move `PROJ/.buildozer` somewhere else?

Rationale: I like keeping my project folders in Dropbox, so they are accessible on all my machines. However, `PROJ/.buildozer` is currently 251 MB (too big). I cannot move it somewhere else and put a symbolic link on it because Dropbox doesn't treat symlinks correctly and pulls in the linked folders :(

I think one should be able to set the location of this folder in the `buildozer.spec` file.

**Edit:**

OK, after diving in the source I found it. It is supported in `buildozer.spec` the following way (example):

```
[buildozer]

builddir = /home/jabba/.kivy_builddirs/kivy_project
```

Tip: such a line should be in `buildozer.spec` in a commented state. This way it would be easier to find and customize.

issue_title: Please upgrade the documentation
issue_body:
This link https://buildozer.readthedocs.org/en/latest/installation.html is outdated.

**Android on Ubuntu 13.10 (64bit)**

`sudo pip install --upgrade cython
sudo dpkg --add-architecture i386
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install build-essential ccache git ia32-libs libncurses5:i386 libstdc++6:i386 python2.7 python2.7-dev openjdk-7-jdk unzip zlib1g-dev zlib1g:i386`

**Installing ia32-libs shows this error**
    
    Package ia32-libs is not available, but is referred to by another package.
    This may mean that the package is missing, has been obsoleted, or
    is only available from another source
    However the following packages replace it:
          lib32z1 lib32ncurses5


Please change it to this or as you please. I just wanted to bring this into consideration.

`sudo apt-get install build-essential ccache git libncurses5:i386 libstdc++6:i386 python2.7 python2.7-dev openjdk-7-jdk unzip zlib1g-dev zlib1g:i386 libgtk2.0-0:i386 libpangox-1.0-0:i386 libpangoxft-1.0-0:i386 libidn11:i386`

issue_title: Rude install behavior
issue_body:
Hi Nathaniel, can you please remove this line 

    install_requires=["numpy"],

from setup.py? It isn't a good idea to have this line here, because then if you use pip or easy_install to install patsy, it will try to upgrade numpy under some circumstances. This is rude and should never happen automatically. In my case, I had 1.8.0-dev on my system (in-place build) and pip tried to ignore it and install 1.7.0 into site-packages.

The same was done for statsmodels. There it was replaced with

     try:
         from numpy.version import short_version as npversion
     except ImportError:
         raise ImportError("statsmodels requires numpy")

issue_title: sum returns wrong shape for arrays with a zero dimension
issue_body:
When summing over an array that contains a dimension with length zero, `numexpr.sum` fails to reduce the dimensions and instead returns a result with the same shape as the input.  This behaviour is inconsistent with core Python:

```
In [142]: z = empty(shape=(0, 10)) 

In [143]: numpy.sum(z, 1).shape
Out[143]: (0,)

In [144]: numexpr.evaluate("sum(z, 1)").shape
Out[144]: (0, 10)
```

issue_title: Add option for "zero aware" quantiles/bins
issue_body: Sometimes a user might want to analyze a factor where positive and negative values are treated separately. Since the amount of positive values might be different than the negative ones (the factor is biased) the `quantiles` option wouldn't do a good job in separating positive from negative values. Currently the only way to separate positive from negative values is to use the `bins` option, providing custom bin ranges. Is this enough or do we want to improve it?

issue_title: Standard Deviation: Sample vs. Population
issue_body:
The code for annual_volatility and the related Sharpe ratio and others uses the numpy `std `function. However, the source code automatically sets `ddof=1`, which if I understand correctly is appropriate if the data is a sample. However, I am using data that is the entire population, so I need to set `ddof` to 0. It would be nice if we could add that as an option in the higher level functions.

Source: http://www.statsdirect.com/help/basics/degrees_freedom.htm

issue_title: Fails with "http://schema.org/" context
issue_body:
Using this toy json-ld from the playground the following code fails:

```
>>> from rdflib import Graph
>>> test_json="""
... {
...   "@context": "http://schema.org/",
...   "@type": "Person",
...   "name": "Jane Doe",
...   "jobTitle": "Professor",
...   "telephone": "(425) 123-4567",
...   "url": "http://www.janedoe.com"
... }"""
>>> Graph().parse(data=test_json, format='json-ld')
<snip long traceback>
ValueError: No JSON object could be decoded
```

This culprit seems to be that the schema.org context cannot be obtained properly.

```
>>> from rdflib_jsonld.util import source_to_json
>>> source_to_json("http://schema.org/")
ValueError: No JSON object could be decoded
```

which seems to be the "fault" of the corresponding function from rdflib

```
>>> from rdflib.parser import create_input_source
>>> create_input_source("http://schema.org/").getByteStream().read()[:50]
'<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/199'
```

It yields the html variant of that page, whereas it should likely do something like this:
https://github.com/digitalbazaar/pyld/blob/master/lib/pyld/jsonld.py#L385
and request 'application/json'  from the server.

Apologies if that assessment is wrong. I decided to report it here and not against rdflib, as this issues seems to be json-focused (not RDF) and schema.org looks like a relevant component of the jsonld ecosystem.

issue_title: Unsigned int Images
issue_labels: [{'id': 5670344, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GalSim-developers/GalSim/labels/base%20classes', 'name': 'base classes', 'color': '0b02e1', 'default': False}, {'id': 83487880, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GalSim-developers/GalSim/labels/feature%20request', 'name': 'feature request', 'color': 'd4c5f9', 'default': False}, {'id': 356320727, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GalSim-developers/GalSim/labels/starter%20projects', 'name': 'starter projects', 'color': 'c2e0c6', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
On branch #654, I fixed a number of problems that we had related to reading in unsigned int Images.  We don't have any unsigned int instantiations for the Image class in C++, so it had been recognizing the incompatibility and converting to float64 (our default catchall if we can't handle the dtype of a numpy array).  

However, for badpix masks, this isn't the right thing to do, so now the code uses the corresponding int type instead.  i.e. uint16 -> int16 and uint32 -> int32.  This works ok, but it would be better if we actually supported uint Images correctly.

issue_title: fa 5.0.2: icons.json/.yml lacks category meta-data
issue_body:
While upgrading a [Font Awesome plugin](https://github.com/TheDiveO/TW5FontAwesome) for [TiddlyWikis](https://www.tiddlywiki.com) I decided to add an icon cheat sheet (kind of interactive gallery) to the plugin demo wiki, in the spirit of https://fontawesome.com/icons?d=gallery.

In this process I noticed that the `icons.json` and `icons.yml` metadata files from the fontawesome-free-5.0.x distribution lack information about the categories. That is, the [Font Awesome icon galery](https://fontawesome.com/icons?d=gallery) has categories like "Accessibility", "Arrows", et cetera -- see the category checklist in the gallery's sidebar.

Would you please be so kind as to add the category metadata to the `icons.json` and `icons.yml` metadata files? This would allow other projects using Font Awesome icons to also offer icon cheat sheets. While the metadata files contain search terms, they lack any category information. In particular, the search terms can't be used (yet) as a substitute for categories -- for instance, while there is a `accessibility` **search term**, there is only one icon that has this search term, and that's the `fa-accessible-icon`. However, the accessibility **category** of the gallery shows 29 icons (for the free fonts).

Can you support a `category` property in the metadata files?

issue_title: Postgresql.write_recovery_conf: FileNotFoundError
issue_body:
Migrated from https://github.com/zalando/patroni/pull/436

# Scenario

- Kubernetes 1.6.0 on GKE
- wal-e backups and basebackups stored on GCS
- env `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` points to account with GCS "Storage Object Admin" role.
- StatefulSet running spilo with patroni 1.2.5, postgresql 9.6, `replicas: 2`
- Empty volume mounted as `/home/postgres/pgdata`
- When creating the second pod, i.e. the error occurs when trying to create a new replica for a cluste with a leader.

# Expected result

patroni should start downloading from basebackup or wal-e from GCS backup in order to not put additional strain on the master node, then start replicating from master. This seems to be supported by patroni, at least in an Kubernetes+AWS

# Actual result

```
2017-04-26 06:30:57,744 INFO: Lock owner: patroni-set-0006-0; I am patroni-set-0006-1
2017-04-26 06:30:57,751 INFO: trying to bootstrap from leader 'patroni-set-0006-0'
wal_e.main   INFO     MSG: starting WAL-E
        DETAIL: The subcommand is "backup-list".
        STRUCTURED: time=2017-04-26T06:30:58.362701-00 pid=7246
oauth2client.transport INFO     Attempting refresh to obtain initial access_token
oauth2client.client INFO     Refreshing access_token
2017-04-26 06:30:59,064 INFO: replica has been created using wal_e
2017-04-26 06:30:59,064 INFO: bootstrapped from leader 'patroni-set-0006-0'
2017-04-26 06:30:59,068 ERROR: Exception during execution of long running task bootstrap from leader 'patroni-set-0006-0'
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/patroni/async_executor.py", line 39, in run
    wakeup = func(*args) if args else func()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/patroni/ha.py", line 134, in clone
    return self.state_handler.follow(node_to_follow, cluster.leader, True)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/patroni/postgresql.py", line 1007, in follow
    return self._do_follow(primary_conninfo, leader, recovery, timeout)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/patroni/postgresql.py", line 1063, in _do_follow
    self.write_recovery_conf(primary_conninfo)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/patroni/postgresql.py", line 899, in write_recovery_conf
    with open(self._recovery_conf, 'w') as f:
FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/home/postgres/pgdata/pgroot/data/recovery.conf'
```
repeated, as patroni is restarted by supervisor.

## Analysis

### Files and directories

From the running postgres-set-0006-1 pod.

#### patroni configuration

- `/home/postgres/postgres.yml` has been created: https://gist.github.com/joar/321652e961754d8362195b3776692185

#### wal-e configuration

```
/home/postgres/etc/wal-e.d/env/WALE_GS_PREFIX: gs://my-postgres-backup/spilo/my-scope/wal/
/home/postgres/etc/wal-e.d/env/GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS: /etc/patroni/secrets/service-account.json
/home/postgres/etc/wal-e.d/env/TMPDIR: /home/postgres/pgdata/tmp
```
`/etc/patroni/secrets/service-account.json` exists and is walid

#### Files in `data_dir`

`/home/postgres/pgdata` exists, but without `pgroot/data`
```
pgdata/
├── lost+found
├── pgroot
│   └── pg_log
│       ├── postgresql-0.csv
│       ├── postgresql-1.csv
│       ├── postgresql-2.csv
│       ├── postgresql-3.csv
│       ├── postgresql-4.csv
│       ├── postgresql-5.csv
│       ├── postgresql-6.csv
│       └── postgresql-7.csv
└── tmp
```

### Trying to create `data_dir` manually

If I create `/home/postgres/pgdata/pgroot/data` manually, I get another error:

```
2017-04-26 09:33:53,787 INFO success: patroni entered RUNNING state, process has stayed up for > than 1 seconds (startsecs)
2017-04-26 09:33:53,847 INFO exited: patroni (exit status 1; not expected)
2017-04-26 09:33:54,850 INFO spawned: 'patroni' with pid 15819
2017-04-26 09:33:55,345 INFO: Selected new etcd server http://10.71.254.134:2379
2017-04-26 09:33:55,353 CRITICAL: system ID mismatch, node patroni-set-0006-1 belongs to a different cluster: 6412988290203516990 != 
2017-04-26 09:33:55,858 ERROR: 
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/patroni/__init__.py", line 134, in patroni_main
    patroni.run()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/patroni/__init__.py", line 110, in run
    logger.info(self.ha.run_cycle())
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/patroni/ha.py", line 946, in run_cycle
    info = self._run_cycle()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/patroni/ha.py", line 903, in _run_cycle
    sys.exit(1)
SystemExit: 1

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/patroni/dcs/etcd.py", line 329, in wrapper
    retval = func(self, *args, **kwargs) is not None
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/patroni/dcs/etcd.py", line 511, in delete_leader
    return self._client.delete(self.leader_path, prevValue=self._name)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/etcd/client.py", line 584, in delete
    self.key_endpoint + key, self._MDELETE, params=kwds)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/patroni/dcs/etcd.py", line 211, in api_execute
    return self._handle_server_response(response)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/etcd/client.py", line 928, in _handle_server_response
    etcd.EtcdError.handle(r)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/etcd/__init__.py", line 304, in handle
    raise exc(msg, payload)
etcd.EtcdCompareFailed: Compare failed : [patroni-set-0006-1 != patroni-set-0006-0]
```

caused by `self.state_handler.sysid` being `''` at [patroni/ha.py#L901](https://github.com/zalando/patroni/blob/1d513e7e04cc0c69113b9a94d37b0296ceae2906/patroni/ha.py#L901).

The `Compare failed : [patroni-set-0006-1 != patroni-set-0006-0]` is curious, does my replica think it's the leader?

issue_title: Calculate "base ressource" for autonomy allowance (APA)
issue_body:
**[La base ressource APA](https://www.legifrance.gouv.fr/affichCodeArticle.do;jsessionid=F4F3E86E4CCC1894740ECAA305CE16E2.tpdila08v_2?idArticle=LEGIARTI000032138608&cidTexte=LEGITEXT000006074069&dateTexte=20161006) =**
+Le revenu déclaré sur la fiche d'imposition (ou de non-imposition)
+Bien ou capitaux ni exploités ni placés (or résidence principale occupé par l'intéressé ou un membre de sa famille)
-Prestation en nature de l'assurance maladie, maternité, invalidité, accident du travail, CMU
-APL et aide personnalisé au logement
-prime de déménagement
-L'indemnité en capital attribuée à la victime d'un accident du travail
-La prime de rééducation et le prêt d'honneur 
-La prise en charge des frais funéraires 
-Le capital décès servi par un régime de sécurité sociale.

Cette base diffère de la base ressource ASPA.
**[Base ressource ASPA](https://www.legifrance.gouv.fr/affichCodeArticle.do;jsessionid=1F80B865FC1EE5F89861C81153623212.tpdila18v_2?idArticle=LEGIARTI000027268441&cidTexte=LEGITEXT000006073189&dateTexte=20161220) =** 
+tous les avantages d'invalidité et de vieillesse dont bénéficie l'intéressé
+des revenus professionnels et autres (incluant revenus des biens mobiliers et immobiliers et des biens dont il a fait donation au cours des dix années qui ont précédé la demande)
-La valeur des locaux d'habitation occupés à titre de résidence principale par l'intéressé et les membres de sa famille vivant à son foyer 
-La valeur des bâtiments de l'exploitation agricole 
-Les prestations familiales
-L'indemnité de soins aux tuberculeux et majoration spéciale du code des pensions militaires d'invalidité et des victimes de la guerre ;
-La prestation complémentaire pour recours à tierce personne et les majorations accordées aux personnes dont l'état de santé nécessite ACTP
-L'allocation de compensation accordée aux aveugles et grands infirmes travailleurs et les avantages en espèces dont les intéressés bénéficient au titre de l'aide sociale
-La retraite du combattant
-Les pensions attachées aux distinctions honorifiques
-L'allocation de logement
-Les allocations de reconnaissance (I et au I bis de l'article 47 de la loi de finances rectificative pour 1999 n° 99-1173 du 30 décembre 1999)
-La mesure de réparation (décret n° 2000-657 du 13 juillet 2000) pour les orphelins dont les parents ont été victimes de persécutions antisémites.

issue_title: Complément Familial Avril 2014
issue_body:
Le barème du Complément Familial a été mis à jour au 1er avril 2014.

Par ailleurs en décembre 2013 a été créé le Complément Familial Majoré pour les familles vivant sous le seuil de pauvreté. Son barème a même été réévalué le 23 avril 2014.
À vue de nez, le Complément Majoré n'existe pas encore dans OpenFisca.

issue_title: Create a new version of the variables endpoint
issue_body:
# Proposition 1

## `/variables` endpoint

List of all variables as an object mapping their variable name to objects containing a `description` property whose value is their `label`, matching the `/parameters` endpoint.

Each variable is then exposed under the `/variable/{{name}}` URL as an object whose values are documented below.

## InputVariable

### Before

```json
{
	"@type": "Float",
	"default": 0,
	"entity": "individu",
	"label": "Salaire de base, en général appelé salaire brut, la 1ère ligne sur la fiche de paie",
	"start_line_number": 479,
	"source_code": "class salaire_de_base(Variable):\n    column = FloatCol\n    entity = Individu\n    label = u\"Salaire de base, en général appelé salaire brut, la 1ère ligne sur la fiche de paie\"\n    set_input = set_input_divide_by_period\n    url = u'http://www.insee.fr/fr/methodes/default.asp?page=definitions/salaire-mensuel-base-smb.htm'\n    definition_period = MONTH\n",
	"source_file_path": "model/revenus/activite/salarie.py",
	"name": "salaire_de_base",
	"url": "http://www.insee.fr/fr/methodes/default.asp?page=definitions/salaire-mensuel-base-smb.htm"
},
```

### After

```json
"salaire_de_base": {
	"description": "Salaire de base, en général appelé salaire brut, la 1ère ligne sur la fiche de paie",
	"valueType": "Float",
	"defaultValue": 0,
	"definitionPeriod": "month",
	"entity": "individu",
	"reference": "http://www.insee.fr/fr/methodes/default.asp?page=definitions/salaire-mensuel-base-smb.htm",
	"source": "https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/master/model/revenus/activite/salarie.py#L479-489"
},
```

## Variable

### Before

```json
{
	"@type": "Float",
	"default": 0,
	"entity": "famille",
	"label": "Allocations logements",
	"start_line_number": 354,
	"source_code": "class aides_logement(Variable):\n    column = FloatCol\n    entity = Famille\n    label = u\"Allocations logements\"\n    url = \"http://vosdroits.service-public.fr/particuliers/N20360.xhtml\"\n    definition_period = YEAR\n\n    def function(famille, period):\n        '''\n        Prestations logement\n        '''\n        apl = famille('apl', period, options = [ADD])\n        als = famille('als', period, options = [ADD])\n        alf = famille('alf', period, options = [ADD])\n        crds_logement = famille('crds_logement', period, options = [ADD])\n\n        return apl + als + alf + crds_logement\n",
	"source_file_path": "model/mesures.py",
	"name": "aides_logement",
	"url": "http://vosdroits.service-public.fr/particuliers/N20360.xhtml",
	"formula": {
		"@type": "SimpleFormula",
		"comments": null,
		"doc": "Prestations logement"
	}
},
```

### After

```json
"aides_logement": {
	"description": "Allocations logements",
	"valueType": "Float",
	"defaultValue": 0,
	"definitionPeriod": "month",
	"entity": "famille",
	"reference": "http://vosdroits.service-public.fr/particuliers/N20360.xhtml",
	"source": "https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/master/model/prestations/aides_logement.py#L354-L367",
	"formulas": {
		"0001-01-01": {
			"source": "https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/master/model/mesures.py#L360-L367",
			"content": "def function(famille, period):\n        '''\n        Prestations logement\n        '''\n        apl = famille('apl', period, options = [ADD])\n        als = famille('als', period, options = [ADD])\n        alf = famille('alf', period, options = [ADD])\n        crds_logement = famille('crds_logement', period, options = [ADD])\n\n        return apl + als + alf + crds_logement\n"
		}
	}
},
```

## DatedVariable

### Before

```json
{
	"@type": "Float",
	"default": 0,
	"entity": "famille",
	"label": "Revenu de référence, basé sur la situation familiale, pris en compte dans le calcul des AL.",
	"start_line_number": 419,
	"source_code": "class aide_logement_R0(DatedVariable):\n    column = FloatCol\n    entity = Famille\n    label = u\"Revenu de référence, basé sur la situation familiale, pris en compte dans le calcul des AL.\"\n    definition_period = MONTH\n\n    @dated_function(stop = date(2014, 12, 31))\n    def function_2014(famille, period, legislation):\n        al = legislation(period).prestations.aides_logement\n        pfam_n_2 = legislation(period.start.offset(-2, 'year')).prestations.prestations_familiales\n        minim_n_2 = legislation(period.start.offset(-2, 'year')).prestations.minima_sociaux\n        couple = famille('al_couple', period)\n        al_nb_pac = famille('al_nb_personnes_a_charge', period)\n        residence_dom = famille.demandeur.menage('residence_dom', period)\n\n        n_2 = period.start.offset(-2, 'year')\n        if n_2.date >= date(2009, 6, 01):\n            montant_de_base = minim_n_2.rsa.montant_de_base_du_rsa\n        else:\n            montant_de_base = minim_n_2.rmi.montant_de_base_du_rmi\n\n        R1 = montant_de_base * (\n            al.r1.personne_isolee * not_(couple) * (al_nb_pac == 0) +\n            al.r1.couple_sans_enf * couple * (al_nb_pac == 0) +\n            al.r1.personne_isolee_ou_couple_avec_1_enf * (al_nb_pac == 1) +\n            al.r1.personne_isolee_ou_couple_avec_2_enf * (al_nb_pac >= 2) +\n            al.r1.majoration_enfant_a_charge_supp * (al_nb_pac > 2) * (al_nb_pac - 2)\n            )\n\n        R2 = pfam_n_2.af.bmaf * (\n            al.r2.taux3_dom * residence_dom * (al_nb_pac == 1) +\n            al.r2.personnes_isolees_ou_couples_avec_2_enf * (al_nb_pac >= 2) +\n            al.r2.majoration_par_enf_supp_a_charge * (al_nb_pac > 2) * (al_nb_pac - 2)\n            )\n\n        R0 = round_(12 * (R1 - R2) * (1 - al.autres.abat_sal))\n\n        return R0\n\n    # cf Décret n° 2014-1739 du 29 décembre 2014 relatif au calcul des aides personnelles au logement\n    @dated_function(start = date(2015, 1, 1))\n    def function_2015(famille, period, legislation):\n        al = legislation(period).prestations.aides_logement\n        couple = famille('al_couple', period)\n        al_nb_pac = famille('al_nb_personnes_a_charge', period)\n\n        R0 = (\n            al.R0.taux_seul * not_(couple) * (al_nb_pac == 0) +\n            al.R0.taux_couple * couple * (al_nb_pac == 0) +\n            al.R0.taux1pac * (al_nb_pac == 1) +\n            al.R0.taux2pac * (al_nb_pac == 2) +\n            al.R0.taux3pac * (al_nb_pac == 3) +\n            al.R0.taux4pac * (al_nb_pac == 4) +\n            al.R0.taux5pac * (al_nb_pac == 5) +\n            al.R0.taux6pac * (al_nb_pac == 6) +\n            al.R0.taux_pac_supp * (al_nb_pac > 6) * (al_nb_pac - 6)\n            )\n\n        return R0\n",
	"source_file_path": "model/prestations/aides_logement.py",
	"name": "aide_logement_R0",
	"formula": {
		"@type": "DatedFormula",
		"dated_formulas": [
			{
				"formula": {
					"@type": "SimpleFormula",
					"comments": null,
					"doc": null
				},
				"stop_instant": "2014-12-31",
				"start_instant": null
			},
			{
				"formula": {
					"@type": "SimpleFormula",
					"comments": "# cf Décret n° 2014-1739 du 29 décembre 2014 relatif au calcul des aides personnelles au logement\n",
					"doc": null
				},
				"stop_instant": null,
				"start_instant": "2015-01-01"
			}
		]
	}
},
```

### After

```json
"aide_logement_R0": {
	"description": "Revenu de référence, basé sur la situation familiale, pris en compte dans le calcul des AL.",
	"valueType": "Float",
	"defaultValue": 0,
	"definitionPeriod": "month",
	"entity": "famille",
	"source": "https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/master/model/prestations/aides_logement.py#L400-L450",
	"formulas": {
		"2015-01-01": {
			"source": "https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/master/model/prestations/aides_logement.py#L440-L450",
			"content": "def function_2015(famille, period, legislation):\n        al = legislation(period).prestations.aides_logement\n        couple = famille('al_couple', period)\n        al_nb_pac = famille('al_nb_personnes_a_charge', period)\n\n        R0 = (\n            al.R0.taux_seul * not_(couple) * (al_nb_pac == 0) +\n            al.R0.taux_couple * couple * (al_nb_pac == 0) +\n            al.R0.taux1pac * (al_nb_pac == 1) +\n            al.R0.taux2pac * (al_nb_pac == 2) +\n            al.R0.taux3pac * (al_nb_pac == 3) +\n            al.R0.taux4pac * (al_nb_pac == 4) +\n            al.R0.taux5pac * (al_nb_pac == 5) +\n            al.R0.taux6pac * (al_nb_pac == 6) +\n            al.R0.taux_pac_supp * (al_nb_pac > 6) * (al_nb_pac - 6)\n            )\n\n        return R0"
		},
		"0001-01-01": {
			"source": "https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/master/model/prestations/aides_logement.py#L430-L440",
			"content": "def function_2014(famille, period, legislation):\n        al = legislation(period).prestations.aides_logement\n        pfam_n_2 = legislation(period.start.offset(-2, 'year')).prestations.prestations_familiales\n        minim_n_2 = legislation(period.start.offset(-2, 'year')).prestations.minima_sociaux\n        couple = famille('al_couple', period)\n        al_nb_pac = famille('al_nb_personnes_a_charge', period)\n        residence_dom = famille.demandeur.menage('residence_dom', period)\n\n        n_2 = period.start.offset(-2, 'year')\n        if n_2.date >= date(2009, 6, 01):\n            montant_de_base = minim_n_2.rsa.montant_de_base_du_rsa\n        else:\n            montant_de_base = minim_n_2.rmi.montant_de_base_du_rmi\n\n        R1 = montant_de_base * (\n            al.r1.personne_isolee * not_(couple) * (al_nb_pac == 0) +\n            al.r1.couple_sans_enf * couple * (al_nb_pac == 0) +\n            al.r1.personne_isolee_ou_couple_avec_1_enf * (al_nb_pac == 1) +\n            al.r1.personne_isolee_ou_couple_avec_2_enf * (al_nb_pac >= 2) +\n            al.r1.majoration_enfant_a_charge_supp * (al_nb_pac > 2) * (al_nb_pac - 2)\n            )\n\n        R2 = pfam_n_2.af.bmaf * (\n            al.r2.taux3_dom * residence_dom * (al_nb_pac == 1) +\n            al.r2.personnes_isolees_ou_couples_avec_2_enf * (al_nb_pac >= 2) +\n            al.r2.majoration_par_enf_supp_a_charge * (al_nb_pac > 2) * (al_nb_pac - 2)\n            )\n\n        R0 = round_(12 * (R1 - R2) * (1 - al.autres.abat_sal))\n\n        return R0\n\n    # cf Décret n° 2014-1739 du 29 décembre 2014 relatif au calcul des aides personnelles au logement"
		}
	}
},
``` 

## Notes

- The `comments` of the formulas is empty for all but 6 items, 5 of which are TODOs, the last one being an improperly-placed label (PR incoming to fix this). We can drop it.
- The `doc` of the formulas is mostly misplaced labels with duplicate date information. We can drop it from the API, the data should be normalised into the labels.
- We are not 100% satisfied with the `source` property name. `href` would be ambiguous since it would make the user leave the realm of the API itself.
- The parsers do not currently expose the individual dated functions source code.
- Many formulas have no start date. We default to `datetime.date.min`. We are not sure whether this information should be added by the API or set as a default for all formulas within each tax and benefits system, with the API simply exposing the data.
- We remove the `class` source code, since all the information it contains is extracted and exposed as properties.

issue_title: issue.permalink() appends  ' - None' to the link
issue_body:
```python
>>> from jira import JIRA
>>> jira = JIRA('https://jira.atlassian.com')
>>> issue = jira.issue('JRA-9')
>>> issue.permalink()
u'https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRA-9 - None'
```

This is due to `Resource.__getattr__` not raising an AttributeError when being asked for the 'child' attribute.

See pull request #21 for a potential fix.

issue_title: Lack of documentation on how to execute bytecode-only Ethereum contracts
issue_body: There is no clear documentation or examples on how to symbolically execute a bytecode-only Ethereum contract. In this case, the ABI should be provided by the user, but there is no public interface to associate it with a bytecode to run.

```
The intended audience of this guide is developers who use Py-ART. For a more
general introduction to Py-ART aimed at users see the User Reference Manual.

This guide provides  documentation for all modules, function, methods, and classes within Py-ART,
both those in the public API and private members.
 
Documentation is broken down by directory and module.
```

issue_title: Issues with authenticated redirects to the same destination host
issue_body:
I've run into an issue w/ requests & requests_kerberos which I don't think we can fix without changes to requests.

We should not reuse an already established kerberos context on new requests. This means, if we're prompted for authentication, then receive a redirect, we shouldn't reuse the previously generated authorization header when following the redirect, even if the redirect is to the same hostname.

I can update our code to do the right thing (index `HTTPKerberosAuth.context` by `response.url` instead of `response.host`, and pop it off of `self.context` in `authenticate_server`.

What we can't do from here is change `requests.Session.rebuild_auth` to remove the Authorization header on a prepared request when following a redirect, even when the redirect is to the same hostname:

The code for `rebuild_auth` only has access to the `prepared_request`, the `response`, and the `session`. There's no way to check if the _type_ of authentication object mandates the Authorization header be stripped or not:

```
 def rebuild_auth(self, prepared_request, response):
        """
        When being redirected we may want to strip authentication from the
        request to avoid leaking credentials. This method intelligently removes
        and reapplies authentication where possible to avoid credential loss.
        """
        headers = prepared_request.headers
        url = prepared_request.url

        if 'Authorization' in headers:
            # If we get redirected to a new host, we should strip out any
            # authentication headers.
            original_parsed = urlparse(response.request.url)
            redirect_parsed = urlparse(url)

            if (original_parsed.hostname != redirect_parsed.hostname):
                del headers['Authorization']

        # .netrc might have more auth for us on our new host.
        new_auth = get_netrc_auth(url) if self.trust_env else None
        if new_auth is not None:
            prepared_request.prepare_auth(new_auth)

        return
```

I looked through the hook documentation and it doesn't look like there's anywhere we can inject ourselves to modify the behavior. Do you have any ideas?

@sigmavirus24 @Lukasa I think I'll need your help on this one.

By the way, I think this is tangentially related to #54, and the reason @danc86 wasn't experiencing his problem with the "latest" requests at the time was because the latest requests happened to include the previous Authorization header on the redirected request. His suggestion to deregister the hook would have caused the latter responses from being mutually authenticated which would have solved his problem, but means he shouldn't trust that response (if he really wanted mutual authentication. If he didn't he could have just disabled it).
