issue_title: Add bias to NonlinearityLayer
issue_body:
For building an auto-encoder via #373, it would be useful to be able to drag out not only the nonlinearity, but also the bias from a layer into a separate layer (so the `UnLayer`/`InverseLayer` can be built to not invert the nonlinearity and bias, but be followed by its own bias and nonlinearity).

We could either add a `BiasLayer` for that, or regard it as part of the `NonlinearityLayer`. I think it wouldn't hurt to add it to the latter.

To be useful for different purposes, the bias should broadcast over all but the second dimension by default (that would be reasonable for dense and convolutional layers), but also be customizable to broadcast over a set of given axes (as in my [`BatchNormLayer` implementation](https://gist.github.com/f0k/f1a6bd3c8585c400c190)). This would also partly address the use case mentioned in #249.

issue_body:
`embed_notebook()` (for R), `py.iplot()` (for Python), and `JupyterPlot()` (for Julia) currently don't work out-of-the-box inside _nteract_ (they work great in vanilla Jupyter notebook however).

There's currently a [workaround](https://github.com/plotly/plotly.py/pull/562#issuecomment-245078317) being proposed for Plotly's Python interface.

To help keep track, here are 3 separate issues in the repos of each language's interface:
- [x] Issue for R: https://github.com/ropensci/plotly/issues/768
- [x] Issue for Python: https://github.com/plotly/plotly.py/pull/562
- [x] ~~Issue for Julia:~~ https://github.com/spencerlyon2/PlotlyJS.jl/issues/90 ( @sglyon grabbed this)

issue_body:
I'm in the process of setting up a debian package archive that allows installing nteract with `apt-get install` on linux distros such as Ubuntu. Also automatic updates.

- [x] I'd like to make nightly packages from the master branch on git available. Does nteract have a culture of merging mostly working code to master (no point in nightly builds if not)? From personal experience, I think yes.

My current thinking:

- `git pull` latest master branch
- `git checkout -b new-package-branch`
- [x] ~~~`npm version patch`? The version number needs to change, but the git tags could be thrown away. This would mean that actual named releases here on github would need to be for example `npm version minor` instead of `patch` like now.~~~  Actually appending the date as -yyyymmdd to whatever the current version number is probably a better solution, since it will be correct semantic versioning, which is required by the packaging system. It would also help with tying the version number back to the version in git. 
- [x] `npm run dist` to create the .deb package
- [ ] copy the .deb to the archive. Debian packages archives are just any ftp/http hosts serving static files. I haven't quite figured out the best way to host all the files.
- [x] update archive metadata and sign it.
- [x] packages are available via regular system updates

---

There is launchpad.net, which is commonly used to host personal package archives, but as far as I'm aware the `electron-builder` process in its current is not compatible with the build process on launchpad. 

I'm trying to figure out if there is a way to use the GitHub release mechanism to host the `.deb`s for the archive (not for nightly builds, but proper builds maybe). I'm pretty sure the answer is yes. If github pages had http redirects you could even host the metadata and have a repo-contained package archive. That could be fun.

---

I've been playing around with this stuff at https://github.com/jarmokivekas/nteract-apt-archive

issue_body:
## Version **6.26.0** of [babel-cli](https://www.npmjs.com/package/babel-cli) just got published.
Branch Build failing 🚨
Dependency babel-cli
Current Version 6.24.1
Type devDependency
This version is **covered** by your **current version range** and after updating it in your project **the build failed**. As babel-cli is “only” a devDependency of this project it **might not break production or downstream projects**, but “only” your build or test tools – **preventing new deploys or publishes**. I recommend you give this issue a high priority. I’m sure you can resolve this :muscle:
Status Details - ✅ **continuous-integration/travis-ci/push** The Travis CI build passed [Details](https://travis-ci.org/nteract/nteract/builds/265304612?utm_source=github_status&utm_medium=notification) - ❌ **continuous-integration/appveyor/branch** AppVeyor build failed [Details](https://ci.appveyor.com/project/nteract/nteract/build/1.0.3299)

issue_title: Explorer seems to not be removed
issue_body:
Hello,

So I was testing tracer on a non-CGC binary (`fauxware`), and got the `program did not behave correctly, expected only one path` error right after the first step (1). Printing out the simgr stashes gives me 0 `active` 0 `deadended` but 1 `found` (2), but we never use that `found` stash in tracer.

 Apparently we do use `Explorer` for Linux binaries in tracer to find out the entry point. But the `found` stash has indeed got unstashed right after `Explorer` returned.

From (1) and (2), I really think that `Explorer` is always used in `simgr.step`, despite the call to `remove_tech` in the end of `explore`.

issue_title: qrconnect_url encoding issues
issue_body:
I am using wechatpy to add wechat login capability to a website, and I found that WeChat's servers refuse to accept the URLs generated by WeChatOAuth.qrconnect_url.

After some investigation, I found that this method does not encode URLs correctly.

WeChat servers will accept the URLs after changing:

        redirect_uri = quote(self.redirect_uri)

to

        redirect_uri = quote(self.redirect_uri, safe=u'')

Wanted to pass this along to the team and other users who are unable to present their users with the login QR code.
-gary

issue_title: Reading ganga version from .gangarc in 6.1.12
issue_body:
The version stored in 6.1.11 is stored in the x,y.z format rather than the x-y-z format.

Shall we fix the 6.1.12 to be able to read x.y.z as well as x-y-z even though we're storing the format of x-y-z for forward/backward compatibility.
or,
Are we content in asking users to fix their .gangarc files by hand?

Users moving forwards/backwards are already familiar with changing/fixing this but users who just naively update to 6.1.11 and don't pay attention to what version they're on will see 6.1.12 crash on launch.

This is a minor fix from the users perspective but are we wanting to fix this ourselves or have this as a user requirement?

(We could even use this to intentionally break backwards compatibility if we chose in the future i.e. with Ganga-v7 if we ever go down this route.)

issue_title: Reuse of CTR initial counter value
issue_body:
First: love your little utility!

Second: it seems that the the code uses the default value (=1) for the initial counter value for CTR mode for each and every key and encrypted message, which is ... kind of ok.

Reusing the same initial counter value with the same key is bad, but credstash seems to use a new unique key for each encrypted secret it stores. That particular usage pattern doesn't introduce any vulnerability for the same counter value. 

However, any possible future changes of credstash or other possible applications that would use the encrypted secrets in DDB, which would use the same key to encrypt another secret, would have to change that practice and should use a different initial counter values.

Furthermore, any audit of the code will have security engineers cringe and they will have to spend additional effort to ensure that keys and counter values are not reused together.

Looking at the code, it feels that the changes to initialize the counter with a random value, and to store that value with the meta-data in the DDB-record, would not change the code significantly. This could even be implemented in a backwards compatible way (no record value for the initial counter value would default the value to 1).

Are you open to change the code to accommodate unique random initial counter values per key and per encrypted secret?

This seems not ideal, since I'd imagine function featurizer would often be used in a multiplefeaturizer context, but we're going to need to revisit how to flexibly handle feature_labels if we want to fix it.

issue_title: one_of Not Working as Expected
issue_body:
All,

I am trying out a really simple intent parser on Python 3.4. I have defined a simple intent like so:

```{python}
from adapt.engine import IntentDeterminationEngine
from adapt.intent import IntentBuilder
import json

engine = IntentDeterminationEngine()

engine.register_entity("fastfood", "FoodType")
engine.register_entity("show", "Command")
engine.register_entity("what", "Question")

options = IntentBuilder("OptionsForLunch")\
    .require("Command")\
    .optionally("FoodType")\
    .build()

engine.register_intent_parser(options)

for intent in engine.determine_intent("show fastfood"):
    print(json.dumps(intent, indent=4))
```

This works just fine:

```
{
    "Command": "show",
    "intent_type": "OptionsForLunch",
    "confidence": 0.9230769230769231,
    "FoodType": "fastfood",
    "target": null
}
```

However, I would like to specify that either a `Command` or a `Question` are needed for this intent. So I defined the following:

```{python}
from adapt.engine import IntentDeterminationEngine
from adapt.intent import IntentBuilder
import json

engine = IntentDeterminationEngine()

engine.register_entity("fastfood", "FoodType")
engine.register_entity("show", "Command")
engine.register_entity("what", "Question")

options = IntentBuilder("OptionsForLunch")\
    .one_of("Question", "Command")\
    .optionally("FoodType")\
    .build()

engine.register_intent_parser(options)

for intent in engine.determine_intent("show fastfood"):
    print(json.dumps(intent, indent=4))
```

This does not print out any matching intents. Am I using the `one_of` function incorrectly? As far as I can tell, I am using it the same way this [test](https://github.com/MycroftAI/adapt/blob/b9acd5c53645c2570cbe982f1cd4e203bf748009/test/IntentTest.py#L40-43) uses it.

Any help would be greatly appreciated, thanks!

issue_title: Add mechanism to select tests based on Pulp version
issue_body:
Some tests are only applicable to a particular Python version. Let's add some mechanism that allows users to run only tests which apply to a particular Pulp version or a range of Pulp versions. What should this look like?

One option is to use the `skipIf` decorator/function provided by unittest. It lets you write code like [this](https://docs.python.org/3/library/unittest.html#skipping-tests-and-expected-failures):

```python
class MyTestCase(unittest.TestCase):

    @unittest.skip("demonstrating skipping")
    def test_nothing(self):
        self.fail("shouldn't happen")

    @unittest.skipIf(mylib.__version__ < (1, 3),
                     "not supported in this library version")
    def test_format(self):
        # Tests that work for only a certain version of the library.
        pass

    @unittest.skipUnless(sys.platform.startswith("win"), "requires Windows")
    def test_windows_support(self):
        # windows specific testing code
        pass
```

Of particular interest is that `self.skipTest()` can be executed at runtime. There will assuredly be cases where we want to determine whether to skip a test at run-time instead of being able to determine that in advance - maybe in a `subTest()` context manager.

Another option is to set attributes directly on test methods and use nose or nose2's test selection mechanism. See [here](http://nose2.readthedocs.org/en/latest/plugins/attrib.html). It lets you write code like this:

```python
import unittest

class Test(unittest.TestCase):

    def test_fast(self):
        pass
    test_fast.fast = 1
    test_fast.layer = 2
    test_fast.flags = ['blue', 'green']
```

And you can then select tests from the command line, like so: `nose2 -v -A fast`.

The upshot of using unittest's `skipIf` decorator/method and similar decorators/methods is that they're compatible with just about any test runner out there. You can run the test suite with unittest, unittest2, py.test, nose, nose2, etc, and the test suite will behave identically. In addition, using methods/decorators has a very small namespace footprint, and you get to choose which tests should be executed at run-time.

The upshot of using something static like nose{,-2}'s test method attributes is that you get to easily select which tests should be run from the command-line. That's handy - I'd like to be able to say "run all tests that apply to Pulp 2.7 against my Pulp system" (and other similar statements?), and that's veeeery easy to do with nose{,-2}. (This use case is still doable with unittest's `skipIf` decorators/functions. It just may take a little more work - maybe the introduction of helper functions or another environment variable.)

issue_title: Taking action limits into account in PPO/TRPO/ACKTR.
issue_body:
This is more of a question than an issue. I noticed that in the implementation of the above-mentioned algorithms, action limits are not taken into account. Environments handle this clipping internally, so no errors will appear, but this brings us to situations where the algorithm in it's training batch has inputs that were not necessarily applied within the environment.

For example, let's say the upper limit for an input is 1 and the applied input (given from the algorithm) is 5. What the environment will experience is input 1, due to it's internal clipping, but the algorithm will in it's training batch have data with action equal to 5. 

Intuitively it makes sense that the algorithm will learn how to deal with this, but I am wondering if using the information of exactly what action is applied would be beneficial? Additionally, we can think of applying the action clipping even before adding noise (since noise doesn't really do anything if the mean is already out of limits). For example, DDPG handles this nicely with tanh outputs before applying the noise, and with clipping applied afterwards.

issue_title: User-defined Initial Values for scalars and arrays
issue_body:
Since all scalars and arrays are initialized with the same value (0.23), the following code will run faster than expected:
```
(U_op - y[j]) / R
```

since `U_op - y[j]` will be zero and the division will take less cycles to complete.

A solution would be, to make the initial values random or have the user define them.

issue_title: Creating database sessions per query?
issue_body:
Using SQLAlchemy and my current process of creating database sessions revolve around a context manager and a session factory.

AKA in a RESTful API I would do something like this:

    def someroute():
        with session_factory() as session:
            ...do some controller stuff with the session...
            return result

Since Graphene/GraphQL seems to wrap a lot of the magic around querying, how do I inject the session I create dynamically on a query/mutation request?

It seems like the model expected here is to have a persistent global database session (instead of a factory)? Am I correct in assuming this?

P.S. I originally opted for this model since I wanted to contain all database side-effecty stuff in one python module instead of having to hook into my backend request teardown loop to handle cleaning up sessions.

issue_title: debug-toolbar doesn't work correctly with django-hosts
issue_body: Today I discovered that django-debug-toolbar doesn't work correctly on subdomains with django-hosts library. I am adding the debug-toolbar urls also for the subdomain, but the problem is the url for the ajax request is created with a template tag like: {% url 'djdt:render_panel' %} and it doesn't contain the host parameter, so it defaults to www in my case, and that's forbidden because of access control. I'm not sure what's the correct way to resolve this problem, maybe creating a template tag for the url creation which could also take the current host into account, but that doesn't seem right to me.

issue_title: [Feature request]
issue_body:
When you are comparing different execution of similar models, the only way to know, for example, the hyper-paramaters used is naming the directory where session is written.

It would be nice to have a way to set some metadata that can be retrieved by tensorboard. This way it could be possible to filter the execution based on this metadata. Or at least it could be possible to know the execution details or other information saved in the metadata.

Ideally the metadata would be saved by Tensorflow but it could be saved in a well known format and retrieved by Tensorboard.

issue_title: 0.1.36 breaks template validation on our templates
issue_body:
Hi,

We tried 0.1.36 this week and discovered the template validation fails on some of our templates despite them passing the cloud formation api validation check. The issues crop up in the userdata sections and seem to be caused by the use of fn::join's and a mixture of strings and Refs/Fn::GettAtt  

e.g. 

"if [[ $ALLOCATION_ID ]]; then ${AWSCMD} ec2 associate-address --instance-id ${AWS_INSTANCE_ID} --allocation-id ${ALLOCATION_ID} --allow-reassociation --region ", { "Ref" : "AWS::Region" }, " --cli-connect-timeout 60 --cli-read-timeout 60", "|| ERROR_EXIT 'Failed to set EIP.'\n",

changing to

"if [[ $ALLOCATION_ID ]]; then ${AWSCMD} ec2 associate-address --instance-id ${AWS_INSTANCE_ID} --allocation-id ${ALLOCATION_ID} --allow-reassociation --region ", { "Ref" : "AWS::Region" }, " --cli-connect-timeout 60 --cli-read-timeout 60 || ERROR_EXIT 'Failed to set EIP.'\n",

resolves it but it's only in 0.1.36 cfn-sphere it's an issue, everything else works as expected. 

Jonathan

issue_title: Stop using packman for Ruby gRPC client generation
issue_body:
Derived from #69:

Currently Ruby gRPC client pipeline use packman to generate gRPC files. However, that does not fit with the design of gcloud-ruby, where a package contains all of hand-written layers, Gapic-generated files, and gRPC-generated files.
That is Cloud APIs only, but there are no strong reasons to split packages for Gapic files and gRPC files in Ruby.

Because of that, we should stop using packman for Ruby's client pipeline. Instread, similar to Java, we should use protoc_tasks we already have.

Also, from https://github.com/googleapis/artman/issues/69#issuecomment-242172115,

> Currently we get protoc from linuxbrew here: https://github.com/grpc/homebrew-grpc
> That site indicates that the linuxbrew method is deprecated for all languages except C/C++. 

Therefore, Ruby will use grpc-tools rubygem (https://rubygems.org/gems/grpc-tools).

Cc: @bjwatson @geigerj

issue_title: Make it clear that the call may fail with the INVALID_ARGUMENT status code
issue_body:
What:
Wrap the gRPC INVALID_ARGUMENT error with a pythonic VALUE_ERROR. We can do this with a special attribute that we consistently use for the wrapped gRPC error.

Why:
Be more Pythonic.

See https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/issues/15

issue_title: Feature - Retrieve a transaction ownership chain using the asset ID
issue_body:
* bigchaindb-driver version: 0.2.1
* Python version: 3.5.1 
* Operating System: OS X

### Description

Currently the only way to retrieve a transaction is by ID. However there is no way to retrieve a transaction ownership chain using the driver.

BigchainDB already has a get_transactions_filtered() method but doesn't allow both CREATE and TRANSFER operations to be searched at the same time. The operation would be sent as 'ALL' or a list of transactions to include. Given a created asset ID as the transaction ID return the CREATE transaction as well as any TRANSFER transactions.

issue_title: Could  we have binned over sampling for regression?
issue_body:
I am working on a regression task, in my case predicting a percentage which is continuous. If I use RandomOverSamler it does run, but it does this by re-sampling every float value to have the same number as in the most common value.

This doesn't always make sense as the unique values may not be evenly spread throughout the range of possible values. As in this example:

```python
y = np.array([1, 1, 1, 0.9, 0.8, 0])
X = y.reshape(-1, 1)

out = RandomOverSampler().fit_sample(X, y)
pd.Series(out[1]).value_counts()
```

gives

```
0.9    3
0.8    3
0.0    3
1.0    3
```

I think it could be nice to have a system like the `bins` parameter in `matplotlib.pyplot.hist`, so you can say how many equally spaced bins you want to split the data into, or give the bin boundaries. Then using those bins, you can count the number of entries in each bin, and oversample a given bin by randomly picking one of the entries from inside it.

issue_title: Implement merge magic
issue_body:
**This is mostly a brain dump, probably need to split this into more specific tickets**


Here are my thoughts on what this could look like:     

 * Allow certain keys to be suppressed by subsequent layers by using some kind of sentinel value, like `<<UNSET>>` or something.  Use case:  Disabling a transformer defined in the upstream layer. So instead of `TRANSFORMS-class=` being shown in the output, the entire key is removed from the destination.)
 * Allow stanzas to be suppressed with some kind of special key value token.  Use case:  Removing unwanted eventgen related source matching stanza's like `[source::...(osx.*|sample.*.osx)]` instead of having to suppress individual keys (difficult to maintain).
 * Allow a special wildcard stanzas that globally apply one or more keys to all subsequent stanzas. Use Case:  set `index=os_<ORG>` on all existing stanzas in an inputs.conf file.  Perhaps this
   could be setup like

        [*]
        index=os_prod
           --  or --
        [<<COPY_TO_ALL>>]
        index=os_prod
   Assume this will apply to all currently-known stanzas.  (Immediately applied, during the merging process, not held and applied after all layers have been combined.)
 *  Possibly allow list augmentation (or subtraction?) to comma or semicolon separated lists.

        TRANSFORMS-syslog = <<APPEND>> ciso-asa-sourcetype-rewrite
            -- or -- 
        TRANSFORMS-syslog = <<REMOVE>> ciso-asa-sourcetype-rewrite

   Possible filter operators include:

        <<APPEND>> string           Pure string concatenation
        <<APPEND(sep=",")>> i1,i2   Handles situation where parent may be empty (no leading ",")
        <<REMOVE>> string           Removes "string" from parent value; empty if no parent
        <<ADD(sep=";")>> i1;i2      Does SET addition.  Preserves order where possible, squash dups.
        <<SUBTRACT(sep=",")>> i1    Does SET reduction.  Preserves order where possible, squash dups.

   Down the road stuff:        (Let's be careful NOT to build yet another template language...)

        <<LOOKUP(envvar="HOSTNAME")>>               <<-- setting host in inputs.conf, part of a monitor stanza
NOTE:  This is possibly bad or misleading example as HOSTAME maybe on a deployer not on the SHC member, for example.

        <<LOOKUP(file="../regex.txt", line=2)>>     <<-- Can't thing of a legitimate use, currently
        <<RE_SUB("pattern", "replace", count=2)>>   <<-- Parsing this will be a challenge, possibly allow flexible regex start/stop characters?
        <<INTROSPECT(key, stanza="default")>>       <<-- Reference another value located within the current config file.

It may be nice to set these up and register them via decorators.  Make all filter calls align with Python function calling norms (like what Jinja2 does)

     @register_filter("APPEND")
     def filter_op_APPEND(context, sep=","):
         # context.op       name of the filter, "APPEND" in this case.
         # context.stanza   name of the current stanza
         # context.key      the key who's value is being operated upon
         # context.parent   original string value
         # context.payload  raw value of the current key, without magic text (may be blank)

Allow for standard python style function argument passing; allowing for both positional and named parameters (that is `*arg` and `**kwargs`)

issue_title: Cannot call parent method when use multiple inheritance with pybind11::class_
Wrap exception hierarchy across modules
Using `py::keep_alive<N, P>()` may cause `internals.patients[nurse]` to grow without bounds
issue_body:
Looking at some of the internals, it looks like `return_value_policy::reference_internal` is only "activated" when an instance is created:
https://github.com/pybind/pybind11/blob/48e1f9a/include/pybind11/cast.h#L552

However, `py::keep_alive<N, P>()` looks like it is always activated:
https://github.com/pybind/pybind11/blob/48e1f9a/include/pybind11/cast.h#L552
https://github.com/pybind/pybind11/blob/48e1f9a/include/pybind11/attr.h#L442
https://github.com/pybind/pybind11/blob/48e1f9a/include/pybind11/pybind11.h#L1470
https://github.com/pybind/pybind11/blob/48e1f9a/include/pybind11/detail/class.h#L289

A simple fix would be to add a simple check:
```
#include <algorithm>
...
auto& p = internals.patients[nurse];
if (std::find(p.begin(), p.end(), patient) != p.end()) ...
```

This would impact performance a little, but would prevent a packrat problem for long(er)-running Python programs.

issue_title: implicitly_convertible
issue_body:
Hi,

Not really an issue, but, you don't have another place to have these conversations.

This is a really useful feature. I was thinking of the following scenario not necessarily captured.

Imagine you have a C++ object with an iterator range constructor 

``Foo::Foo( Iterator begin, Iterator end) { ..}``

Now whenever you have a type that is also a range over ``Iterator::value_type`` you can offer a conversion from that type to Foo.

I suppose this can also be solved by offering an explicit Range constructor for Foo, e.g.

```
template< typename Range>
Foo( const Range & r){ ... }
```

Then you can always implicitly convert? 

However, is it certainly seems possible to codify ``implicit_range_convert<A,B>(){}`` which glues conversion code in to the iterator range constructor.

This would have the value that you could imagine ``implementing implicit_range_covert<B>() {}`` which then automatically glues all known valid range types..

Thoughts?

issue_title: Revoke old certificate on renewal
issue_body:
Currently `certbot v0.8.1` does not revoke the previous certificate upon renewal with `certbot renew`. I have checked the source code a little bit and also verified this by testing using the actual client. I believe this behavior has not been implemented because it can cause problems with certain automation setups, however, I think it should be implemented and initially be available using a flag, and then, maybe, at some point, be the default. 

Some comments on the proposal above:
* From a security perspective, it makes sense to have the old certificate revoked, since it would not be used any more and there's no reason to have it as valid.
* Most (if not all) browsers do not check for revocation information ([Can you visit this?](https://revoked.grc.com/)) therefore this might not make any difference currently.
* Since most people currently use `certbot renew`, the amount of revoked certificates will increase, placing further load in any CRLs / OCSP Servers that may be used by the CA. Currently the percentage of revoked certificates [is 0.4% for Let's Encrypt](https://plot.ly/~letsencrypt/9/daily-activity/#plot).
* If someone fails to reload their web server after the renewal, they will theoretically serve revoked certificates until they do. 

This is not a high priority issue, it is actually a feature request, and very few people will use this initially, however I am posting this issue so we can track the progress of this.

issue_title: `process_xml` doesn't correctly parse `icon` in all cases
issue_body:
I've run into an issue where certain xml structure that contains `icon` and `cartridge_icon` does not parse correctly.  Take for example the xml from the recently added test case [here](https://github.com/pylti/lti/blob/master/tests/test_tool_config.py#L77).

```python
>>> CC_LTI_OPTIONAL_PARAMS_XML = b'''<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
... <cartridge_basiclti_link xmlns:blti="http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsbasiclti_v1p0" xmlns:lticm="http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imslticm_v1p0" xmlns:lticp="http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imslticp_v1p0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xmlns="http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imslticc_v1p0" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imslticc_v1p0 http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/lti/ltiv1p0/imslticc_v1p0.xsd http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsbasiclti_v1p0 http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/lti/ltiv1p0/imsbasiclti_v1p0p1.xsd http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imslticm_v1p0 http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/lti/ltiv1p0/imslticm_v1p0.xsd http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imslticp_v1p0 http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/lti/ltiv1p0/imslticp_v1p0.xsd">
...   <blti:title>Test config</blti:title>
...   <blti:description/>
...   <blti:launch_url>http://www.example.com</blti:launch_url>
...   <blti:secure_launch_url>http://www.example.com</blti:secure_launch_url>
...   <blti:icon>http://wil.to/_/beardslap.gif</blti:icon>
...   <blti:vendor/>
...   <cartridge_icon identifierref="BLTI001_Icon"/>
... </cartridge_basiclti_link>
... '''
>>> from lti.tool_config import ToolConfig
>>> config = ToolConfig.create_from_xml(CC_LTI_OPTIONAL_PARAMS_XML)
>>> config.icon
>>> config.icon is None
True
>>> 
```

The issue appears to be the use of `in` used to check tag names of xml.  Presumably this is used to get around the fact that tag names include the namespace information.

```python
>>> icon_node.tag
'{http://www.imsglobal.org/xsd/imsbasiclti_v1p0}icon'
>>> 
```

This leads to issues when tag names are substrings of other tag names.  For example in the case of `icon` and `cartridge_icon` the config's icon is correctly set when it encounters the `icon` tag, but then overwritten when `parse_xml` encounters the `cartridge_icon` tag.

As this is potentially a larger change I thought I would raise an issue here first.  I would like to propose the use of lxml.etree.QName to check exact matches of the local tag name.  The existing tag name checks in `process_xml` would be changed to something like:

```python
from lxml.etree import QName
def _is_tag(node, name):
    return QName(node).localname == name

...

if _is_tag(child, 'icon'):
    self.icon = child.text

...
```

There is some evidence that this has been accounted for in the past using a serious of if/elif blocks. e.g.

```python
            if 'secure_launch_url' in child.tag:
                self.secure_launch_url = child.text
            elif 'launch_url' in child.tag:
                self.launch_url = child.text
```

That approach could be taken here as well, but it seems like this may warrant a more general solution.  I'm happy to provide a PR for this change if you would like to move forward.

issue_title: support multiple workerTypes
issue_body:
Currently we have a single workerType, and we get a set of poll-task-urls for that workerType, and we poll them in order for tasks.

Instead, we can have a tuple of workerTypes, and populate their poll-task-urls, and poll them for tasks.  We brainstormed a few ways we could do this.  If we have workerType A and workerType B, and they each have high, middle, and low priority queues (in reality I think we have 5? 6? levels):

* the workerTypes tuple is ordered, and we search all the workerType A queues before proceeding to the workerType B queues.  This would be good if we want to prioritize all the workerType A tasks (e.g., autophone perf tests) higher than anything else, but would be poor if the workerTypes are equal or similar priority.

* the workerTypes tuple is ordered, and we search the highest priority queue for workerType A, then the highest priority queue for workerType B, then the middle priority queue for workerType A, and so on.

* poll each queue async, and build a single list of tasks to claim.  Take the oldest request from the highest priority queue that has tasks.

As long as we have tests for the above (ideally including integration tests) and we have the right scopes for all workerTypes, I think this works.

We'll need this for scriptworker to be able to support Autophone, but this could be useful elsewhere.

issue_title: JSONRPCClientReplyError: replacement transaction underpriced
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

We have been getting `JSONRPCClientReplyError: replacement transaction underpriced`.

This happens in multiple tests. It has happened in:

- [test_new_netting_contract](https://gist.github.com/LefterisJP/590866cd904df439daf9d364f69eb39b)
- [test_settlement](https://gist.github.com/LefterisJP/99cde3078ee517e62fd4aba4343119bb)
- others

## Discussion and Path to Eventual Solution

We are still investigating why this happens. Geth has [two](github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/blob/master/core/tx_pool.go#L51) [places](github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/blob/master/core/tx_pool.go#L463) in the tx_pool code where they are using that error.

It's emitted only when another transaction with the same nonce as the previous transaction is emitted and the `gas price bump` is not met.

Geth has the possibility to define the gas price bump as a command line argument `--txpool.pricebump` so we are also playing with that possibility [here](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/pull/787/files)

Ofcourse the question should also be why do we try to use the same nonce for a pending transaction in order to replace it in the first place??


## Tasklist

- Investigate, find why this happens and fix it

issue_title: Solidify the channel closing/settling strategies
issue_body:
As seen in https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/pull/276 the current methods for closing and settling a channel are not well defined and some thinking. To sum up, at the moment there are cases where a participant can close a channel without a transfer while transfers have been made requiring his counterparty to present 2 different transfers to the channel in order to settle.

Let us use this issue in order to decide on and solidify the different ways to close and settle a channel.
Once that is decided I can implement in in #276.

## Current Channel Closing Functions

### `close(first_transfer, second_transfer)`
Allows closing a bidirectional channel by presenting the latest transactions from both participants. At the moment no checks happen on the transfers ... which allows you to abuse it by providing two different transactions by the same participant.

### `closeSingleTransfer(transfer)`
Allows closing a channel  by presenting the latest transaction. At the moment no checks for who this transaction comes from are made .

### `closeWithoutTransfer()`
Allows closing a channel that was unused. But ... can be abused and can also be called on a channel where transfers happened since the contract has no way to know about it before closing.

### `updateTransfer(transfer)`

After the counterparty has closed the channel this function gives you the ability to dispute the closing of the channel by presenting a more recent transfer .... but as things currently stand just like `close()` this should have the possibility of adding 2 transfers to allow challenging in the case of the counterparty lying and closing with an older transaction state on their part.


## Target Channel Closing Functions

### `close(our_transfer, their_transfer)`
Allows closing a bidirectional channel by presenting the latest transactions from both participants. 
The function should also:
    - check that `our_transfer` comes from the `msg.sender` and that it is a channel participant
    - check that `their_transfer` comes from the other participant

### `closeSingleTransfer(transfer)`
Allows closing a channel that was used only unidirectionally by presenting the latest transaction.

### `closeWithoutTransfer()`
Allows closing a channel that was unused.

### `disputeTransfer(their_transfer, our_transfer)`

After the counterparty has closed the channel this function gives you the ability to dispute the closing of the channel by presenting a more recent transfer for them (`their_transfer`). We also need the ability to present `our_transfer` there too in case the counterparty used `closeWithoutTransfer()` on the channel. This is to facilitate for [this](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/pull/276#issuecomment-264309757) scenario where `closeWithoutTransfer()` is called while transfers have happened both ways in a single-funded channel.

Having a `disputeTransfer(their_transfer)` only during the above scenario would result in a failure at settling because of the an insufficient netted balance for the transfer and the deposit inside the contract would not be enough to do the token transfer.

Please present your ideas on what our `Target Channel Closing Functions` should be.

issue_title: RPC calls for blocknumber and discovery should be cached
issue_body:
Some way of doing less RPC calls for getting the current block number and discovery information must be devised.

Regarding discovery, every time a `raiden` protocol packet is sent from a node to another node, a new RPC call for discovery is made (translates `raiden` address to host:port) in those places:

https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/raiden_protocol.py#L96
https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/raiden_protocol.py#L224

Regarding `block_number` there's lots of usage in the _run loops inside the greenlets created by `task.py`.

One approach to fix the problem is to add caching with time-to-live, which I did for a throughput demo. Commit here:

https://github.com/utzig/raiden/commit/92bf68da3005cb37024b921a70f4620b1fb8e33b

PS: This was a very simple implementation of a possible solution, but it's efficient enough to bring a single 3 node cluster with mediated transfers from 15 transfers/s up to 70 transfers/s without the `LogListenerTask`refactoring as suggested on #233. Probably not a good solution for a production product but works well enough for throughput demo. Some better architecture needs to be devised probably on the RPC client itself.

issue_title: Multiples validator only works for Ints
issue_body:
The `Multiples(Ints)` validator checks if a give value is a multiple of some other value (the `divisor`). 

This validator only works for `Ints` and there is no reason for this. 

A perfectly valid example would be requiring for values to be multiples of 20ns. 

The current value would then be valid `40e-9` as this would return 0.0. Currently this will raise an error as 40e-9 (which is a multiple of 20e-9) raises an error as it is not an Int. I propose dropping the constraint of it having to be an Int.

```
from sklearn.metrics import f1_score

def f1(preds, targs, start=0.17, end=0.24, step=0.01):
    with warnings.catch_warnings():
        warnings.simplefilter("ignore")
        return max([f1_score(targs, (preds>th), average='micro')
                    for th in np.arange(start,end,step)])
```
Best regards
Michael

issue_title: TPOTClassifier.set_params doesn't follow scikit-learn estimator API
issue_body:
Typically, calling `old_estimator.set_params(new_param=foo)` will copy all the other parameters from the old estimator, and only update `new_param`. Currently, TPOT will `__init__` with the kwargs passed to `new_param`, and use the default or everything else.

## Context of the issue

See http://scikit-learn.org/stable/developers/contributing.html#estimators and http://scikit-learn.org/stable/developers/contributing.html#get-params-and-set-params.

## Process to reproduce the issue

```python
clf = TPOTClassifier(random_state=0)
clf.set_params(n_jobs=-1).random_state
# output is None, the default random_state for TPOTClassifier
```
## Expected result

```
>>> clf.set_params(n_jobs=-1).random_state
0
```

## Possible fix

Hopefully just delete the custom `set_params` :) That may not be possible though, as it looks like there's some parameter handling logic in the `__init__`. That logic would need to be either moved to fit (and transform) or a helper method.

issue_title: torchvision.transforms cannot handle certain transformations
issue_body:
I'm trying to replicate the ResNet paper, so I need the following transforms

- Subtract per-pixel-mean from all pixels in all channels (this can lead to negative values)
- `Pad(4)`
- `RandomHorizontalFlip()`
- `RandomCrop([32, 32])`
- `ToTensor()`

The problem is that transforms 2-4 only work on `PIL.Image`, while transform 1 must happen on `np.array` or `torch.Tensor` because of the negative values. I can't convert back and forth inside of the pipeline since that would cut off the negative values, so I'm stuck.

It would be great if there were versions of at least the spatial transforms which work on numpy arrays. As far as I can see, some of them convert the PIL image to array anyway.

issue_title: Object ID search box in web UI doesn't work.
issue_body:
When I paste an object ID into the object ID search box in the web UI, I see this error. Looks like we're missing an import. Looks like the problem was introduced in #731.

```python
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
NameError                                 Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-2-19f3a0f9b806> in handle_submit(sender)
      8 
      9 def handle_submit(sender):
---> 10     pp = pprint.PrettyPrinter()
     11     pp.pprint(ray.global_state.object_table(object_search.value))
     12 

NameError: name 'pprint' is not defined
```

cc @alanamarzoev

issue_title: Worker output not redirected to stdout if redirect_output=False
issue_body:
This illustrates the problem:

```python
import ray

ray.init()

@ray.remote
def f():
    print("Hi")
    return None

ray.get(f.remote())
```

It was introduced here: https://github.com/ray-project/ray/pull/646

issue_title: Failure in the test of plasma store failure.
issue_body:
The test

```
python test/component_failures_test.py ComponentFailureTest.testPlasmaStoreFailed
```

seems to be failing in this log https://s3.amazonaws.com/archive.travis-ci.org/jobs/212596022/log.txt.

I can reproduce this locally by running this in a loop, e.g.,

```
for i in {1..100}; do python test/component_failures_test.py ComponentFailureTest.testPlasmaStoreFailed; done
```

issue_title: [Dataframe] Shallow Copy does not work currently
issue_body:
All Dataframe copies are done as deep copies. This functionality is hard to support given immutability of data in the object store.

```
In [1]: import pandas as pd

In [2]: df = pd.DataFrame({'col1': [1, 2], 'col2': [3, 4]})

In [3]: df
Out[3]: 
   col1  col2
0     1     3
1     2     4

In [4]: df2 = df.copy(deep=False)

In [5]: df
Out[5]: 
   col1  col2
0     1     3
1     2     4

In [6]: df2
Out[6]: 
   col1  col2
0     1     3
1     2     4

In [7]: df['col2'] = [8, 9]

In [8]: df
Out[8]: 
   col1  col2
0     1     8
1     2     9

In [9]: df2
Out[9]: 
   col1  col2
0     1     8
1     2     9
```

issue_title: [tune] S3 sync from workers
issue_body:
### Describe the problem
Currently the tune S3 syncer only syncs from experiment logs from the head node. This is a problem if you have custom logs that you put in $CWD, since those are not sent to the head node.

We should also sync those, separately. Note that this requires granting worker nodes S3 access.

cc @vlad17 @richardliaw

issue_title: [rllib] Support checkpoint/restore of observation filter state
issue_body: Currently, observation filter state (e.g. in policy gradient) is not managed by rllib and is lost on checkpoint/restore.

issue_title: [Question] Download youtube live stream from the start(seek)
issue_body:
Hi, is there a way to download a live video from youtube from the start instead of from the current time (i.e. seek to the start)?

I am able to download with the command line **streamlink "URL" best -o out.ts**, but it starts downloading from the current time but i want the complete video.

I've read the command-line documentation and i couldn't find any "seek like" option. Am I missing something here or there's no way to do this using this software?

issue_title: Plugin: funimation.com VOD
issue_body:
### Checklist

- [ ] This is a bug report.
- [x] This is a plugin request.
- [ ] This is a feature request.
- [x] I used the search function to find already opened/closed issues or pull requests.

### Description

[Funimation Downloader NX (nodejs based funimation.com downloader)](http://kametsu.com/topic/54534-funimation-downloader-nx-nodejs-based-funimationcom-downloader/?page=4#comment-527466)

[FunimationNOW Kodi Add-on](http://forum.kodi.tv/showthread.php?tid=304479)

This one seems to be fairly straightforward and we could get what we need here:

[script.video.funimationnow/funimationnow.py at master · Protocol-X/script.video.funimationnow](https://github.com/Protocol-X/script.video.funimationnow/blob/master/resources/lib/modules/funimationnow.py)

Easier to give an example right off the bat, go for the `iframe` part @ line 427:

[https://www.funimation.com/shows/dragon-ball-super/universe-9s-basil-the-kicker-vs-universe-7s-majin-buu/](https://www.funimation.com/shows/dragon-ball-super/universe-9s-basil-the-kicker-vs-universe-7s-majin-buu/)

Grab that link to their Brightcove player first:

[https://www.funimation.com/player/256245/?bdub=0](https://www.funimation.com/player/256245/?bdub=0)

Either grab these guys @ line 33 directly:

`"alphaExternalId": "DBSSIM0079", "screenshots": ["https://d132fumi6di1wa.cloudfront.net/exp/FunimationStoreFront/1590221/Japanese/1590221_Japanese_2a30c000-f9d1-e611-80c5-0017a4776c06.m3u8"], "huluId": "", "textTracks": [{"src": "https://d132fumi6di1wa.cloudfront.net/FunimationStoreFront/1590221/Japanese/1590221_Japanese_2930c000-f9d1-e611-80c5-0017a4776c06.vtt", "kind": "subtitle", "type": "Full", "language": "en", "label": "English"},`

Or go for the `url` @ line 48 instead since that's even neater, we could simply get all `m3u8` links to the **entire season** by replacing `256245` with the corresponding `"experienceId"` for each episode:

[https://prod-api-funimationnow.dadcdigital.com/api/source/catalog/title/experience/256245/](https://prod-api-funimationnow.dadcdigital.com/api/source/catalog/title/experience/256245/)

Getting pretty much the same stuff afterwards:

`"sources":[{"purchase":"","huluId":"","screenshots":["https://d132fumi6di1wa.cloudfront.net/exp/FunimationStoreFront/1590221/Japanese/1590221_Japanese_2a30c000-f9d1-e611-80c5-0017a4776c06.m3u8"],"alphaExternalId":"DBSSIM0079","showAds":false,"textTracks":[{"src":"https://d132fumi6di1wa.cloudfront.net/FunimationStoreFront/1590221/Japanese/1590221_Japanese_2930c000-f9d1-e611-80c5-0017a4776c06.vtt","kind":"subtitle","type":"Full","language":"en","label":"English"}`

Strip `/exp` whenever we see that:

> https://d132fumi6di1wa.cloudfront.net/FunimationStoreFront/1590221/Japanese/1590221_Japanese_2a30c000-f9d1-e611-80c5-0017a4776c06.m3u8

Add anywhere between `_Layer1` and `_Layer9` right before `.m3u8`

issue_title: Output stream delay of Twitch.tv streams
issue_body:
### Checklist

- [ ] This is a bug report.
- [ ] This is a plugin request.
- [x] This is a feature request.
- [x] I used the search function to find already opened/closed issues or pull requests.

### Description

Original feature request: streamlink/streamlink-twitch-gui#416

I'm not entirely sure if this is part of the HLS/m3u8 specs, but Twitch includes stream timestamps in their HLS playlists, so that a playback delay to the broadcaster can be calculated by their web player (see the [`created_at`](https://dev.twitch.tv/docs/v5/reference/streams/#get-stream-by-user) property of stream records from the Twitch API). I'm aware that there still can be an additional delay added by the local player when using Streamlink.

It would be useful for applications using Streamlink to read the current timestamp or even the calculated delay, if possible. This could be outputted as debug log message or a pipe (specified by a parameter) whenever a new playlist file (or stream chunk?) is being read.

If this data can't be used, maybe all unrecognized m3u8 tags could be written as debug messages.

### Example

```
#EXTM3U
#EXT-X-VERSION:3
#EXT-X-TARGETDURATION:5
#ID3-EQUIV-TDTG:2017-02-23T01:56:22
#EXT-X-MEDIA-SEQUENCE:21823
#EXT-X-TWITCH-ELAPSED-SYSTEM-SECS:87306.456
#EXT-X-TWITCH-ELAPSED-SECS:87299.595
#EXT-X-TWITCH-TOTAL-SECS:87330.456

```

Result from User testing summary:

![screen shot 2017-10-06 at 12 06 52 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11495372/31313816-5765fd58-aba3-11e7-96db-9497c4c304b6.png)
First time use, new user don't know how to get to Git UI. So, we found high fail rates for 2 operations:

Correct Action: double-click My Project folder;
1st time fail rate: 42.3%; 2nd time fail rate: 19.23%
Mistakes:
--Single clicked (selected) My Project then navigated to Git tab (6),

--User wanted a representation that a directory in the file browser is a git repo (2) 
--Clicked refresh button in Git (1)

2: Navigate to Git UI
Correct Action: click Git tab on left
1st time fail rate: 26.9%, 2nd time fail rate: 11.5%
Mistakes: 
--Using command line to navigate (2), 
--initially clicked on the top-level "Git" menu (4) 
--Clicked refresh icon within Git tab (1)

Cause: 
Unfamiliar with Jupyter; 
Common user habits (user saw top-level Git first, and try to click) 

Solution:
Provide link to Git UI through top-level “Git” menu;
Use image to tutorial;
Enlarge the font of the tutorial instructions;

issue_title: Remove all `sys.exit(1)` from within the code
issue_body: Pulling the plug just because something unexpected happend is not a calm response and makes error tracking/debugging tough. It also makes it impossible to include that code in anything else knowing that it could just exit. We should replace the occurrences of `sys.exit(...)` with proper exceptions which we should deal with in the `main` function.

issue_title: Test Failure at test_local_json.py::test_local_json_parse
issue_body:
When `make travis_test` is run, the titled test fails. 

The test loads the pato.json ontology from `tests/resources/`, and then asks for `"PATO:0001374"`. In the ontobio/networkx code, this amounts to taking the built networkx graph, called `graph` and:
```
graph.node["PATO:0001374"]
```
This raises a KeyError.

In a python interpreter, I printed out the networkx nodes with:
```
for n in graph:
    print(n)
```

And the list returned was full of ids that looked like:
```
OBO:PATO_0001336
OBO:PATO_0000004
OBO:PATO_0000488
OBO:PATO_0000487
```

Repeating the test asking instead for OBO:PATO_0001374 (`graph.node["OBO:PATO_0001374"]`), I get a returned result.

This suggests that the mapping of ids somehow changed, but the networkx version has been what it is for a while. This issue looks like the weirdness how rdflib is weird about prefixes, but I don't think rdflib is being used.
.

issue_title: Clarify how to fully clean widget state when updating versions
issue_body:
I have some example notebooks in my custom widget repository that I want to update to use the latest version of the JS package whenever I release a new version, but I find it difficult to get it right on first try and often end up with old widget states still in my notebooks.

From experimenting I think the required steps are:
1. Open notebook
2. Clear cell output
3. Clear widget state
4. Refresh page
5. Execute cells
6. Save widget state

(this seems consistent with [this comment](https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/ipywidgets/issues/1692#issuecomment-326632419))

Step 4 seems to be required -- perhaps some widget state persists in the client-side JS even after running "clear widget state". Is this intended? If so, can it be made clearer that this step is necessary, because running "clear widget state" sounds like it should be enough?

I wasn't sure what minimal example would help but I can try to put one together if needed.

issue_title: Unable to use both a built-in widget and a custom widget in JupyterLab
issue_body:
## Background
I've been working on adding JupyterLab ipywidget support (In addition to the working notebook ipywidget support) to the this WIP plotly.py branch (plotly/plotly.py#942), and I'm running into a problem.

## Problem (Bug?)
A custom-built local widget can't be displayed in the same jupyterlab notebook as a built-in widget provided by `@jupyter-widgets/jupyterlab-manager`

## Simplified Example
Steps to reproduce
1) Create a fresh conda environment for Python 3.6. Install jupyterlab into the environment using pip
```
$ conda create -n jupyterlab_widget python=3.6
$ source activate jupyterlab_widget
$ pip install jupyterlab
```
2) Follow jupyter widget cookiecutter instructions [here](https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/widget-cookiecutter) to create a new widget named `jlwidget`
```
$ pip install cookiecutter
$ cookiecutter https://github.com/jupyter/widget-cookiecutter.git
```
3) Build the widget
```
$ cd jlwidget/
$ python setup.py build
$ pip install -e .
```
4) Install widget for notebook
```
$ jupyter nbextension enable --py widgetsnbextension
$ jupyter nbextension install --py --symlink --sys-prefix jlwidget
$ jupyter nbextension enable --py --sys-prefix jlwidget
``` 
5) Test in classic notebook. Create an `IntSlider` in the first cell and a `HelloWorld` widget in the second cell. Both display properly.
```
$ jupyter notebook
```
![screen shot 2018-04-17 at 6 47 05 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15064365/38865265-40ac3dda-420b-11e8-9e05-2e9ff7b8ca7a.png)

6) Build/install for jupyterlab. Note: my understanding is that `@jupyter-widgets/jupyterlab-manager` needs to be installed to view any widgets in JupyterLab and to view the `IntSlider`
```
$ jupyter labextension install @jupyter-widgets/jupyterlab-manager
$ jupyter labextension install js
```
6) Test in jupyterlab. Execute the same notebook shown above. The `HelloWorld` widget displays fine but there is an error displaying the `IntSlider`
```
$ jupyter lab
```
![screen shot 2018-04-17 at 6 56 02 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15064365/38865673-82ac23ac-420c-11e8-8728-d0e3ea05746a.png)

Here is the error in the chrome developer console

![screen shot 2018-04-17 at 6 58 13 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15064365/38865759-c8280f4a-420c-11e8-9ad8-98e63236c61b.png)

7) Disable the `jlwidget` widget, and repeat the experiment. Now the `IntSlider` displays fine, and the `HelloWorld` widget does not (as I expected)
```
$ jupyter labextension disable jlwidget
```
![screen shot 2018-04-17 at 7 01 21 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15064365/38865907-44801470-420d-11e8-9c4e-fe796b8ff30e.png)

![screen shot 2018-04-17 at 7 02 42 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15064365/38865939-603b623c-420d-11e8-9b8c-f5047e5d131c.png)

issue_title: Remove batching from `catch_signals`?
issue_body:
I was just looking at [`catch_signals`](https://trio.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference-io.html#trio.catch_signals), and it occurred to me maybe we can improve on its funky batching API.

The original motivation was [the same as for `UnboundedQueue`](https://trio.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference-hazmat.html#unbounded-queues): since we have no way to put backpressure onto signals as they arrive, we nudge users to handle a whole batch at once.

One alternative I considered were to use a regular `UnboundedQueue`, which has the problem that its memory use actually be unbounded, which feels wrong given that signals are coalescable and thus actually can be queued with bounded memory. The other was to store incoming signals in a `set`, and then pop an element each time. The problem with this is that it can lead to a kind of starvation where signals of type A keep coming in and getting popped, while a signal of type B sits in the set indefinitely without being reported. (For example, a policy that would guarantee this worst-case behavior would be to always report the lowest-number pending signal.)

What I realized now though is that there's another option: we could use the `set`-type approach, but with a policy that's guaranteed to cycle through all the different signals in bounded time. For example, if on each iteration we report the signal with the lowest number *that is strictly larger than the one we reported last time* (wrapping around as necessary), then we'd guarantee that any incoming signal was reported within 64 event loop iterations at worst (at least on Linux which has 64 signals, but other OSes are similar). (And of course in practice it would be much, much quicker, since you never receive all 64 signals at once.)

Probably the nicest way to accomplish this would be to use an `OrderedDict` for queueing incoming signals, like we do for `TrioToken.run_sync_soon(..., idempotent=True)` calls.

There's some transition issue here, since this change would be backwards incompatible in a way that we can't warn for. We can either come up with a new name for `catch_signals` and transition to using that instead, or else rip off the bandaid by just changing it.

issue_title: Files with explicit file:// URL scheme do not resolve as I expected on the executor side
issue_body:
If I write `File('a.txt')`, then on the execution side, `inputs[0]` resolves to `a.txt`, a valid filename.

If I write `File('file:///etc/passwd'), then on the execution side, `inputs[0]` resolves to `file:///etc/passwd` which is not a valid filename.

Example code below.

#!/usr/bin/env python3

import parsl
from parsl import File
from parsl import DataFlowKernel
from parsl import DataFlowKernelLoader
from parsl.config import *
from parsl.app.app import bash_app

parsl.set_stream_logger()

c = Config(executors=[ThreadPoolExecutor()])

dfk = DataFlowKernel(config = c)

# workaround, issue #381
DataFlowKernelLoader._dfk = dfk

@bash_app(data_flow_kernel = dfk)
def foo(inputs=[]):
  return ("ls ; echo X{}X ; [ -f {} ] ".format(inputs[0], inputs[0]))

myfile = 'file:///etc/passwd'

future = foo(inputs=[myfile])

print("RESULT: {}".format(future.result()))

issue_title: Results added to appCache before retries exhausted
issue_body:
If an app fails, the exceptions are entered into the appCache before the logic that handles retries. This results in retry attempts being skipped and the first failure being returned, effectively blocking retries.

The task goes through the following state transitions : 
```
     pending --> running --> completed
      ^            |
      |            V
      +--------? failed       
```

We should only memoize when the task has reached the terminal states (completed/failed).

issue_title: Question regarding multithreading
issue_body:
- Windows 10, x64
- Python 3.6.6
- JDK 1.8.0_171

We just stated to use JEP in naivly, when we stumbled quite early about the fact, that a JEP instance cannot be used from multiple threads once instantiated. The scenario is quite simple:

- A stand alone server makes use of a Python lib which is very costly in terms of computing time and memory to create and initilize
- Therefore we need to have it in memory until the JVM process itself is shutdown
- It is created in the main thread but should be used via an REST interface (server by Jetty) which obviously has it's own threads -> BANG!

My approach would be simply to have a singleton around the JEP instance and route all calls through "synchronized" methods of the singleton. Due to the underlying problem we can anyhow create only a single instance of this library so this would be a perfect fit. But, this does not work with JEP, so what could be solutions:

1. Fork JEP and remove the checks in Jep.isValidThread() to return always true. I have done some work with JNI already and would not confirm that multithreading and JNI in general is a problem (as the JEP doc suggessts). This however is the most dirty solution...
2. Wrap the single instance into a threaded on it's own and talk to it using a wrapper and RMI internally. Would work, but still somwhow crude.
3.  I read something about the "shared modules feature", which could solve the problem somehow on Python level. But I was not able to find an example or more description of it, so don't know how to use this. However, just from reading some basic information about it this could be a nice fit as the "one global Python lib instance" is exactly what we requrire and I could still guarantee that only a single thread will access the JEP environment.

The preferred order for solving this would likely be 3, 2, 1, so where can I learn something about this shared module feature and how is it applied.

Best regards,
Soeren

issue_title: Smart-seq2 and multithreading
issue_body:
Hi,

In the Usage Guide is stated that:

> The sorting procedure uses samtools sort and it is expected to be time consumning, because of this, the procedurre is perfomed in parellel by default.

and 

> ..., because of the above mentioned multithreaded call to samtools sort, running several instances of veloctyo run might end up using the memory and cpu of your system and possbily result in runtime errors.

Those statements don't hold true for `run_smartseq2`, right? In fact, `run_smartseq2` doesn't even take the `--samtools-threads` parameter to control the number of threads, showing the error `Error: no such option: --samtools-threads`.

When running `run_smartseq2`, the log information shows one thread only being used:

`Command being run is: samtools sort -l 7 -m 1M -t NOTAG -O BAM -@ 1 -o cellsorted_test.bam test.bam`

This raises another question. Why is a cell sorting step even necessary for Smart-seq2 samples, since these consist of one bam file per cell?

Thanks,

While runnning [keras-preprocessing(master)/image.py/random_channel_shift](https://github.com/keras-team/keras-preprocessing/blob/master/keras_preprocessing/image.py#L182-L194), I thought it was different from the expected channel_shift behavior. I think that the expected channel shift movement is old. #### Original Images(cifar10) ```python from keras.datasets import cifar10 from keras.preprocessing.image import random_channel_shift import numpy as np import matplotlib.pyplot as plt def plot_tiles(images, rows=5, columns=5): pos = 1 for idx in range(rows*columns): plt.subplot(rows, columns, pos) img = images[idx] plt.imshow(img) plt.axis("off") pos += 1 plt.show() (x_train, y_train), (x_test, y_test) = cifar10.load_data() sample_images = x_train[:9]/255 channel_shift_range = 0.3 plot_tiles(sample_images, rows=3, columns=3) ``` #### Latest Channel_Shift Images(cifar10) ```python channel_shift_images_latest = [] for _ in sample_images: channel_shift_images_latest.append(_random_channel_shift(_, channel_shift_range, 2)) channel_shift_images_latest = np.array(channel_shift_images_latest) plot_tiles(channel_shift_images_latest, rows=3, columns=3) ``` [keras-preprocessing(master)/image.py/random_channel_shift](https://github.com/keras-team/keras-preprocessing/blob/master/keras_preprocessing/image.py#L182-L194) #### Old Channel_Shift Images(cifar10) ```python channel_shift_images_old = [] for _ in sample_images: channel_shift_images_old.append(random_channel_shift(_, channel_shift_range, 2)) channel_shift_images_old = np.array(channel_shift_images_old) plot_tiles(channel_shift_images_old, rows=3, columns=3) ``` [keras-preprocessing(old)/image.py/random_channel_shift](https://github.com/keras-team/keras/blob/8532744ad3de309e807ade1b9b6eff9603305b1a/keras/preprocessing/image.py#L178-L199)

issue_title: For MapBox maps, change or remove requirements for Values field
issue_body:
## As a data scientist, I want to put geospatial feature data on a map and show arbitrary fields (attributes) so that I can understand spatial relationships and access individual feature information.
See also [issue #459](https://github.com/ibm-watson-data-lab/pixiedust/issues/459): preserveCols is an interim step, since it is already partially implemented.

### Expected behavior
In the Options dialog, I can choose or list attributes to be displayed in hover popups on the map. In the current dialog layout, the Values box can be used for that.

### Actual behavior
The Values box is restricted to numeric columns only. This makes sense for normal charts but is an arbitrary and inappropriate restriction for maps.

### Steps to reproduce the behavior
Take any geospatial data and use display() to create a MapBox map. It is impossible to drag a non-numeric column into the Values box.

issue_title: require() returns single-element array
issue_body:
When I do something like `var c = require('config.yaml');` I get a single-element array with the YAML-derived data structure as the element. I guess that's more or less as documented, but it seems strange. I would have expected that I'd get just the data structure, with no extra array around it. Having to use a `[0]` to get to the actual data is an annoyance. Is there some point I'm missing?

That is, I'd hoped to get

    {"property1":"value1","property2":"value2"}

but instead I get

    [{"property1":"value1","property2":"value2"}]
